racecards.race_name: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Has plenty of speed in his good pedigree and needs a close look on debut
runners.spotlight: £21,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Kimngrace (5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 98), Tallinski (5f-6.5f including France; 87), Rock Of Estonia (5f-7f including 2yo/Italy; 82) and Baltic State (5f AW; 76); dam 5f AW winner (82); plenty to like on paper and he needs a close look on debut.

runners.owner: Hold My Beer, D Cunha, B Little

prev_owners.owner: Hold My Beer, D Cunha, B Little

runners.comment: Some ability over C&D last month but he needs more improvement back on slow ground
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability dropped to 5f on his second start when fifth of eight over C&D (good to firm) last month; was only beaten around 3l but he needs more improvement on this return to slow ground.

runners.comment: Runner-up at Ripon last month and he's open to more progress on this drop in trip
runners.spotlight: Ran green on Southwell debut in July and he left that form behind when runner-up at Ripon (6f, good; 100-30) last month; that form is not strong but he took a strong hold under prominent tactics and is open to more progress on this drop in trip; in the mix.

runners.comment: Drawn out wide but has claims if he can build on his promising debut fourth at Newcastle
runners.spotlight: 80-1 on Newcastle debut (5f, AW) last Monday but he ran well for a long way before finishing a 3l fourth of 13; drawn out wide on this switch to turf but has possibilities if he can build on that debut run.

runners.comment: Half-brother to numerous winners and he needs watching in market on debut
runners.spotlight: Eleventh foal; half-brother to winners Flying Pursuit (6f/7f including 2yo; RPR 109), Straightothepoint (6f including 2yo; 90), Spirited Guest (5f/6f; 89), Ventura Rascal (7f/1m; 84) and Havana Pursuit (5f/6f; 76); dam 5f winner (including 2yo; 73); has plenty of speed in pedigree and he needs watching in market on debut.

runners.breeder: Mrs S Field, Miss S Field & Mr A Turbitt
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but he sets clear standard on his best form; respected on return
runners.spotlight: Showed clear promise when third at Pontefract (5f, good) and York (5f, good to firm) in his first two starts; never got involved at Carlisle (good to soft) in May and gelded since, but he sets a clear standard on his best form and is a half-brother to a soft-ground winner; respected back from a break.

runners.breeder: Mrs J A Rawding & Miss A J Rawding
runners.comment: Massive price and he finished a remote last of seven on recent Catterick debut
runners.spotlight: Missed the break and was always behind when a remote last of seven on Catterick debut (5f, good; 200-1) last month; left Danny Brooke since then and is best watched on stable debut.

runners.comment: Mixed results in four starts but his best form was on soft and he's not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in his four starts and was beaten 10l at Ripon last time, but his only outing on soft ground resulted in a second at Carlisle (6f) in June; has each-way claims if he can recapture that form and this return to 5f should be fine.

runners.comment: Well held in two 6f runs and has lots to find on this drop in trip
runners.spotlight: Beaten 11l or more in two 6f novice events and latest was at Thirsk 11 days ago; has lots to find on this drop in trip and nurseries will be more suitable.

runners.comment: Big prices and has struggled in two 6f events, with a best RPR of 35
runners.spotlight: Out of a useful 7f/1m winner but he's struggled in two 6f events, with a best RPR of 35; can only be watched on this drop in trip.

runners.comment: Half-sister to a 2yo winner and she needs checking in market on debut
runners.spotlight: 23,000gns foal, 10,000gns breeze-up 2yo; third foal; half-sister to 7f AW/7.4f turf 2yo winner Liberty Bird (RPR 72); dam German 7f Listed winner (95), out of maiden half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Screen Star (dam of Cheveley Park winner Lumiere); sire A'Ali should inject some speed into the mix and she needs checking in market on debut.

runners.comment: Has lots of juvenile winners in her pedigree and market should guide on debut
runners.spotlight: 3,000euros yearling; eighth foal; sister to 5f winner Let's Go Hugo (including 2yo; RPR 73), half-sister to five winners including Ready Freddie Go (5f including 2yo; 99), Scuzeme (5f/6f; 83) and Roaring Rory (5f/6f including 2yo; 69); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (87); has lots of juvenile winners in her pedigree and market should guide on debut.

racecards.verdict: The returning BRAVO HOTEL has leading claims if he can get back near his best and he's a half-brother to a soft-ground winner.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Bravo Hotel, 7/2 Quantum Power, 5/1 Finnic, 7/1 Brave Traveller, 8/1 Jack Rabbit Slims, 10/1 Noelies Dream,  Sands Of Josepi, 12/1 Arctic Ali, 16/1 Let Fly Skye, 50/1 Long Shot,  Muvable, 100/1 Fordham Flyer

racecards.race_name: Topmark Solutions Facilities Management EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Strong finish to win a 5f fillies' novice at Wolverhampton 12 days ago; can do better
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her Newbury debut when keeping on powerfully to win a 5f fillies' novice at Wolverhampton 12 days ago; still looked green there and she's open to further progress; penalty demands more but she's a nice prospect.

runners.comment: 2nd over C&D on debut; easy AW win 11 days later; strong claims despite her penalty
runners.spotlight: Promising second over C&D on her debut (good to firm) last month and she was unextended to win a 5f maiden at Southwell 11 days later; strong contender despite the 7lb penalty and slower ground.

runners.comment: Modest form when third on debut in May but bred to leave that well behind
runners.spotlight: Ran to just a modest level on her debut in May (6f, good) but she was sent off the 2-1 second favourite and is bred to leave that behind in time; not discounted back from her four-month layoff.

runners.comment: 14l behind Moonlight Mirage on recent Wolverhampton debut; hard to recommend after that
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and always in rear in the 5f novice won by Moonlight Mirage at Wolverhampton 11 days ago; can't be recommended on that evidence.

runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners out of a 7.5f winner in Germany; bred to have a bright future
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to winners Local Girl (Italian 7.5f) and Way To Dubai (German 1m/1m1f); dam German 7.5f 2yo winner; one of two interesting newcomers for her owner.

runners.comment: 5f looked inadequate at Wolverhampton 11 days ago; tough task back in novice company
runners.spotlight: 5f looked inadequate on her nursery debut 11 days and although she was staying on late, she looked a tricky ride; needs a big step forward back in novice company.

runners.owner: S Ullyott, T Coyle & P Robinson
runners.comment: Failed to build on debut promise on her second start; improvement essential
runners.spotlight: Debut effort (5f, good) wasn't without hope but she took a backwards step at Southwell two weeks ago (6f); others more convincing.

runners.comment: Finished last in two runs for Kevin Ryan; hooded ahead of her stable debut
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two turf runs for Kevin Ryan (only 5-2 for her debut); makes debut for a new yard (same owner) with a point to prove; hooded today; withdrawn having bolted to post at Newcastle last week.

runners.comment: Her three siblings are all useful winners; powerful 2yo stable; a newcomer of note
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to UAE 6f dirt winner Welcome Dream (RPR 86), half-sister to winners Nad Alshiba Green (5f including 2yo; 94) and Amirat Alward (6f including 2yo/AW; 88); dam unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Queen Kindly; represents a powerful stable and although she has a useful standard to aim at, she needs a market check.

racecards.verdict: Ophelia Grace sets a useful standard but the ground is a query and LADY YOUMZAIN could make a winning debut.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/5 Ophelia Grace, 11/4 Tea Queen, 11/2 Fast Generation, 13/2 Lady Youmzain, 25/1 Tanaka, 33/1 Mademoiselle Belle, 50/1 Peggy Boo, 66/1 Jungle Juice Jojo

racecards.race_name: EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Game effort to win over C&D last month; form not franked since; vulnerable under penalty
runners.spotlight: Second to a useful filly on her Carlisle debut and put that experience to good use when seeing off 12 rivals over C&D (good to firm) 24 days ago; showed plenty of resolve in the final 100 yards on that occasion but the form has taken some knocks since and she'll need a step forwards if she is to defy a penalty.

runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall
runners.comment: First foal of a useful 7f 2yo winner; interesting newcomer, especially if backed
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 7f 2yo winner (RPR 90), herself from a good family; starts out in a race lacking depth and market confidence would look significant.

runners.comment: Dam a well-bred 1m2f winner; starts out at a realistic level; big run would be no surprise
runners.spotlight: Third foal of a well-related 1m2f winner (RPR 72); starts out at a realistic level and a big run would be no surprise.

runners.comment: Never dangerous on Goodwood debut (1m) but was backed and can leave that behind in time
runners.spotlight: Attracted support (12-1 into 13-2) ahead of her Goodwood debut last month (1m, good to firm) but she was slowly away and couldn't make any impression; should leave that behind in time though and she remains of interest.

runners.comment: Poor form in her two runs; hard to recommend
runners.spotlight: Poor form in her two runs; hard to recommend.

runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership
runners.comment: Latest second at Ayr was an improved display but she looked wayward; capable of better
runners.spotlight: Improved form when second of seven at Ayr last month (7f, good) but she looked wayward, hanging left in the straight and tiring in the final furlong; can still do better but she'll need to keep straighter from a wide stall now going right handed.

runners.comment: Second run better than her debut; more to come but nurseries may prove more suitable
runners.spotlight: Second run (7f, good) was better than her debut (5f, good) and she remains capable of better; that progress may come once tackling nurseries though.

runners.comment: Inconsistent profile; career best needed if she is to feature this time
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut and again at Chelmsford 18 days ago; two runs in between weren't without hope but she will need a career best if she's to feature today.

runners.comment: Over 8l to find with Lady Hornblower on last month's C&D run; slower ground needs to help
runners.spotlight: Modest form in her two runs, the latest when over 8l behind Lady Hornblower over C&D 24 days ago; improvement required for the slower ground.

racecards.verdict: Caim is a newcomer of note but COTTON BUD should have learned plenty from her Goodwood debut and can take a big step forwards.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Lady Hornblower, 4/1 Caim,  Elizabetty, 9/2 Cotton Bud, 13/2 Trotamunda, 8/1 Sweet Love, 12/1 Resdev Time, 16/1 Miss Potomac, 100/1 Aly Louise

racecards.race_name: Topmark Solutions National EV Bay-Marking Specialist Nursery Handicap

runners.owner: D Bishop, S Bland & T Coyle
runners.comment: Shaped well at York on second start; appeals as a likely improver now handicapping
runners.spotlight: His York sixth in July (7f, good) was comfortably his best effort and marked him down as one to note for a nursery campaign; needs improvement to defy this opening mark but it would appear likely.

runners.comment: Easy win on nursery debut (1m, AW); less good latest but still has time on his side
runners.spotlight: Upped 2f for his nursery debut at Kempton last month (1m) and made all to record a wide-margin success; beaten favourite at Chelmsford ten days later and now has a 3lb higher mark to deal with; not fully exposed at the trip though and returning to turf shouldn't be an issue.

runners.comment: A neck behind Dunkeld Dreamer at Thirsk; 1lb better off but may not reverse the placings
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to nurseries when a neck second behind Dunkeld Dreamer at Thirsk 11 days ago (1m, good) 1lb better off today but the winner wasn't doing much in front and the placings may not be reversed.

runners.comment: Best run came on good to soft; should appreciate this test and she's open to improvement
runners.spotlight: Promise in two of her three starts and her best effort came on the one occasion she's tackled slower than good; this trip should suit and she is open to improvement.

runners.comment: Has shown some promise, including here, but needs improvement for the step up in trip
runners.spotlight: Second over 7.4f here (good to firm) on debut and looks favourably handicapped on that effort; has not built on it since, including when third at Wolverhampton on last month's nursery debut (7f), but there is encouragement for this trip on pedigree; ground slower than good would be an unknown.

runners.comment: 2-3 in nurseries; should appreciate this stiff test but slower than good would be a query
runners.spotlight: 2-3 since moving into nurseries, the wins coming over 7.4f here (good to firm) and 1m at Thirsk 11 days ago (good ground); should appreciate the greater emphasis on stamina today and she may not have finished progressing just yet; unraced on slower than good.

quotes.race: Rowland Family Day Out Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: She's very tough. I spoke to Karl Burke beforehand and he said Dunkeld Dreamer stays very well, keep the revs up. She was there to be shot at at the four-marker but she dug deep. She was loving the battle, every time one came to her she found more - Shane Gray, jockey.

runners.comment: Improvement is essential now stepping up in trip for her nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three novice/maiden runs at up to 7.4f; bred to stay but she will need a big leap forwards to trouble some of these.

racecards.verdict: An interesting race despite the small field. SINGARDA's York sixth in July marked him down as one to keep a close eye on in nurseries.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Mr Moonshine,  Dunkeld Dreamer, 5/1 Isambard Kingdom,  Fairydale, 6/1 Singarda, 7/1 Ibn Altheeb, 25/1 Due To Shine

racecards.race_name: Westwood Handicap

runners.comment: Return to a 0-55 will help, as will the step back up in trip, but needs more off this mark
runners.spotlight: Bolted up in first-time blinkers at Salisbury in July but paid for it with a 9lb rise in the weights; the return to 0-55 company will help, likewise the step back up from 7.4f, but he has to prove this mark is within range.

runners.owner: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr
runners.comment: Usually goes with the pace, so stall 15 is a concern, and his latest flop needs ignoring
runners.spotlight: Last three wins have all come in May, latterly over C&D off 2lb higher this spring; usually goes with the pace, so stall 15 is an obvious concern, and his latest flop as favourite round Redcar, a course he likes, will need forgiving.

quotes.race: Supporting Weston Park Cancer Charity Handicap
quotes.quote: Copper and Five has been running quite keen in behind so we decided to just let him stride over a bit shorter and it suited him down to the ground, he's battled right to the line. My saddle slipped on the way to the start and we just re-saddled him at the start - Joanna Mason, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's a good old boy who carries condition and doesn't like having his ears touched! He wins most years, including three in 2021, and should win this year because he's well handicapped. A mark of 57 is as low as he's ever been. 09-04-25

runners.owner: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club
runners.comment: Won easily on Tapeta last week and is well treated under a penalty; leading claims
runners.spotlight: Won easily on Tapeta last week and is well treated under a penalty; the drop from middle distances looked to suit that evening and finally getting off the mark at the 23rd attempt will only have boosted her confidence.

runners.comment: Has never run badly round here and knows how to win; soft ground raises stamina concerns
runners.spotlight: Finished third in this race last year, when drawn wide off a 3lb lower mark; has never run badly round here and knows how to win but does most racing over shorter and soft ground raises stamina concerns.

runners.comment: Still to finish placed and will need another forward step if he's to get off the mark
runners.spotlight: Dam won over 1m2f and he fared better for tackling this longer trip, in first-time cheekpieces, a fortnight ago; still to finish placed and will need another forward step if he's to get off the mark though.

runners.comment: Back on his last winning mark but stall 14 isn't ideal for one happiest on the sharp end
runners.spotlight: Best known for his Tapeta work but has won here (soft) and is back on his last successful mark; stall 14 isn't ideal for one happiest on the sharp end however.

quotes.race: Spreadex Sports Injury Time Loser Insurance Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Back From Dubai broke well and I just let him use his stride and he's toughed it out to the line - Jordan Williams, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Needs to settle better than last time if she's to see it out on soft ground
runners.spotlight: Held a few times, including over C&D, since a Carlisle win off this mark in the spring; will need to settle better than last time if she's to see it out on soft ground.

stable_tour.quote: I think she was a bit unlucky on her return at Newcastle at the end of March as she got a little bit squeezed out, so I'm hoping that with a run under her belt she'll be seen to better effect next time. She has entries at Haydock and Doncaster on Saturday. 23-04-25

runners.comment: The years seem to be catching up and never been quite this far, so stamina is a worry
runners.spotlight: The years seem to be catching up and he again never threatened round Catterick, a course he's always liked, last time; started out as a sprinter and has never been quite this far, so stamina is a worry.

quotes.race: "Irish Day" @thirskraces Saturday 18th May Maiden Auction Stakes
quotes.quote: Obee Jo's a huge horse with a great temperament. He'll be a nice horse one day - Tim Easterby, trainer.

runners.comment: Big prices and well beaten all starts for two different yards; first-time headgear tried
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten all starts for two different yards; first-time headgear will need to make a huge difference.

runners.comment: Inconsistent but has responded well to cheekpieces of late; one to consider
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but has responded well to cheekpieces, matching his Ayr 0-50 win when third in handicap company off this mark a fortnight ago; may well be dropped in again from stall 12 and is one to consider.

prev_owners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith

runners.comment: Ex-Irish gelding who often blows the start; continued to prove inconsistent for this yard
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who often blows the start and has continued to prove inconsistent for his current yard; pulled hard when not seeing out 1m2f last time (Tapeta) and won't mind soft ground.

quotes.race: Golf At Gowran Park Claiming Race
quotes.quote: Weegeebear actually jumped slow and I went to plan B straightaway, down the rail. I was able to scoot up their inside turning the bend and got a really nice position. He travelled into the straight really well and quickened up. He's hit the line strong. He had been slowly away at Bellewstown last time. When you jump slow around Bellewstown it's hard to get into it, we struggled all the way really. I'm glad he's gone and done it today - Adam Caffrey, jockey.

runners.comment: Well handicapped now; again no sign of the cheekpieces worn for last summer's two wins
runners.spotlight: May have needed his turf return a month ago, back from a layoff, and is well handicapped now; again no sign of the cheekpieces worn for last summer's two wins (soft/good), though, and Jimmy Henry may be his trainer's best chance.

runners.comment: Longstanding maiden over various distances who hasn't finished placed since his 3yo debut
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden over various distances who hasn't finished placed since his 3yo debut; will do well to change that.

runners.comment: Clear second to a subsequent winner last time; that gave him something to build on
runners.spotlight: Maiden who proved this trip was within range when a clear second to a subsequent winner in July; that gave him something to build on for a yard also responsible for Kodebreaker.

runners.comment: More in race not run to suit at Catterick last week; longer trip not assured on pedigree
runners.spotlight: Bettered her AW efforts when showing more in a race not run to suit at Catterick last week; longer trip isn't assured on pedigree but she's lightly raced and open to improvement.

racecards.verdict: Perhaps JIMMY HENRY can get off the mark, having shown this trip within range at Leicester last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/1 Hashtagnotions, 6/1 Copper And Five,  Jimmy Henry, 7/1 Elettaria, 8/1 Back From Dubai,  Kodebreaker,  Oilisa, 10/1 First Encounter, 12/1 Ballitore, 16/1 Rose Of New Jersey,  Obee Jo,  Weegeebear, 66/1 William Marshal, 100/1 Wath Court

racecards.race_name: We Are Christmas Party Central Handicap

prev_owners.owner: Pall Mall Partners And Sue Hopgood

runners.comment: Won this in 2022 (soft) and 2024 (good to firm); fair chance of making it 4-8 over C&D
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2022 (soft) and last year (good to firm) for a yard (saddles Tiriac too) that also had the winner in 2020; bit higher in the weights this time but ran well enough in Listed company here last time to think she has a chance of making it 4-8 over C&D.

stable_tour.quote: She's an absolute sweetheart, I love her to bits. She's very honest and genuine and she's easy to train. The lack of early pace got her beaten at Pontefract last time and she has run well all year without winning, which is quite frustrating. She needs a proper end-to-end gallop over a stiff 5f. 14-08-25

runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
runners.comment: Arrives in form but also off a career-high mark; may be the one to lead up the far rail
runners.spotlight: Arrives in fine form, having just been edged out of it on Tapeta four weeks ago under Warren Fentiman, but also off a career-high mark; may be the one to lead up the far rail from stall 2 if that's been the trend to this point.

runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner (arguably unlucky not to make it five latest); soft ground no help
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner (arguably unlucky not to make it five latest) who's enjoyed a good season; up to a career-high mark now, though, and today's surface will work more in the favour of others (withdrawn several times on account of soft/heavy ground).

quotes.race: betting.sites.ltd Promotional Sign Up Offers Restricted Maiden Stakes (Horses In Bands C&D) (GBB)
quotes.quote: The further Emeralds Pride went, the better she got. She could go that extra furlong and it might just be a little bit smoother for her over the seven furlongs. Connor Beasley, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: She won again at Beverley on Sunday, landing her second race there this season over 5f. She did it very well, having broken better than in previous races. She's in good form but we'll see what the handicapper does. 28-08-24

runners.owner: R J H Limited And Ruth Carr
runners.comment: Not as good now but remains consistent given his age; recent rain a negative
runners.spotlight: Long time since he won on turf and isn't as good now but remains consistent given his age; nothing wrong with his latest Haydock second off 2lb lower but has been taken out several times on account of good to soft or slower ground in the past.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: It was very easy. I think five furlongs is going to be the key to him, I don't think he stays six really. We've bounced and done things right. I'm not sure if it was much of a maiden but he gave me a nice feel today. He was behind some proper horses in maidens. Even though he wasn't getting near to them it was proper, nearly top-class, form in the book - Donnacha O'Brien, rider

stable_tour.quote: Won a couple of times last year including at the Racing League. He wants five furlongs in fast conditions and there's a nice race for him at York in May before the Epsom Dash in June. He's capable of popping up in those types of races. 19-04-24

He had a long career with David O'Meara before coming here and I'm pleased to have him because he's a lovely looking sort. He had his first race for us at Newcastle last month, running without any headgear and coming fourth of eight. Headgear will be an option next time he runs. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Hard to place last year but has found his level again this time; soft ground suits
runners.spotlight: Proved hard to place last year but has found his level again this time; disappointing effort as favourite behind two of these in the first-time tongue-tie (retained) at Haydock but is on an attractive mark now and soft ground suits.

quotes.race: British EBF 40th Anniversary Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Changeofmind is very exciting and one we rate a lot at home. He has lots of ability and natural talent. He'll go for the Westow Stakes next month at the Dante meeting. We'll take it one step at a time and keep our feet on the ground - Declan Carroll, trainer.

runners.comment: This is stronger and Connor Beasley, who's often ridden her, partners Emeralds Pride
runners.spotlight: Consistent 3yo who's proven competitive off her revised mark since a Thirsk win (5f, good to soft) off 8lb lower in July; this is stronger, though, and Connor Beasley, who's often ridden her, partners Emeralds Pride.

stable_tour.quote: She's won at Catterick in May and is ready to run again. She ran in the Hilary Needler at Beverley last time but she was a bit keen, so she'll  come back to a flat 5f. She's owned by the Dods Racing Club - we have put four or five horses in the club and people get a lot of fun out of it. 28-08-24

runners.comment: Not look to have any excuses in a first-time hood (retained) behind Alligator Alley latest
runners.spotlight: Pulled away his chances at Windsor in July but didn't look to have any excuses in a first-time hood (retained) behind Alligator Alley last time; can hang left and is some way down the pecking order.

quotes.race: Jumps Free Trial At racingtv.com/freetrial Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Phoenix Of Dreams has been very consistent all summer and had a little blip at Chester, but everyone and every horse can have an off day. It was nice for him to get his head in front - John Quinn, joint-trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart
runners.comment: Stiffer 5f will suit back from a break but he'll need a deal more into a tougher handicap
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo who returns from a two-month break having last been seen finishing comfortably held behind Brazilian Belle at Thirsk; this stiffer 5f will suit but he'll need a deal more into a tougher handicap.

runners.comment: Made it 2-3 over C&D off 4lb lower 18 days ago; has to prove he's as good on slower ground
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 over C&D, on his second run back from a second round of wind surgery, when going in from the same stall off 4lb lower 18 days ago; evidently well suited by fast ground and now has to prove he's as good in these conditions into a better race.

runners.comment: No luck in running last time and is dangerously handicapped; has won on heavy; considered
runners.spotlight: Dual heavy-ground winner whose York victory for Jack Channon in spring 2024 came off a 7lb higher mark; no luck in running last time and is dangerously handicapped now; one to consider.

quotes.race: Betonline At BetGoodwin Cock O'The North EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
quotes.quote: Tiriac ran very well at Newmarket the other day, it was a good horse who beat him there. He's a full-brother to Nastase who won the Rockingham Stakes - Mick Channon, trainer

prev_owners.owner: Mrs J A Wakefield And Partner

runners.comment: Last five wins come over a stiff 5f; unlucky loser at Sandown last Friday; fair claims
runners.spotlight: Last five wins have come over a stiff 5f, including C&D, and he's well treated now; looked an unlucky loser at Sandown last Friday, running on ground slower than good for the first time this year, and holds fair claims.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Sunday Series Sprint Handicap
quotes.quote: Good Earth ran at Goodwood on unraceable ground in the Stewards' Cup and they called the meeting off after that and Yarmouth was on the dead side and today was more on the ground that he likes. We know he jumps the stalls well, but we weren't expecting him to jump that well. We half expected to take a lead of Tom Marquand's horse (Get It) and sit maybe fifth or sixth and come with a run and see how far we have got. I've put him in the Ayr Gold Cup, but it depends on the ground and if it soft that would be a bit stiff. There is a race at York on Saturday but that might come too soon. The Portland would be a race for him and he only just missed getting in last year, but that would be a race for him - Michael Herrington, trainer.

quotes.race: cavani.co.uk The Sartorial Sprint Handicap
quotes.quote: Good Earth loves a stiff five furlongs as he has won at Beverley and run well at Sandown. I think a stiff five probably suits him better than an easy six as he can sometimes over race a bit early. The top horses probably came out of the race which made it a little bit easier for us, but he has done it well. We've got some good races to go to war at.  There is the Sunday Series, and he has won one of them already so we might get greedy and go for another one of them. I've put him in the Stewards' Cup, but that is an easy six with a stiff finish. I'm in the Racing League as well in the Yorkshire team with Leonna Mayor and hopefully she might put me in one of them races as they are worth a good bit - Michael Herrington, trainer.

runners.breeder: Mrs S Field, Miss S Field & Mr A Turbitt
runners.owner: Peter R Ball, Gentech Ltd & Partners
runners.comment: First crack at 5f back from four months off; tended to be all or nothing to date
runners.spotlight: Below par in two runs in the spring and is having a first crack at 5f back from four months off; tended to be all or nothing to date and others have more pressing claims; yard also responsible for Stash The Cash.

runners.owner: HP Racing Stash The Cash & PTR
runners.comment: Spent most of the season running on faster ground than ideal; well treated now
runners.spotlight: Dual winner this time last year (good to soft/soft) who's spent most of the season running on faster ground than ideal, the last twice in cheekpieces; well treated now if he can bounce back but David Allan has opted for Act Of Violence.

stable_tour.quote: He had a few little issues and was very hard to train last year, and he got himself handicapped because of that. But we've been really pleased with him this year and he won well at Catterick first time. 23-04-24

racecards.verdict: Elegant Erin has a fair chance of winning this for the third time in four years but preference is for stablemate TIRIAC.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/1 Good Earth, 6/1 South Parade, 7/1 Emeralds Pride,  Alligator Alley,  Tiriac, 8/1 Elegant Erin,  Hundred Caps, 10/1 Brazilian Belle, 12/1 Changeofmind, 20/1 Phoenix Of Dreams,  Dc Cogent,  Act Of Violence,  Stash The Cash

racecards.race_name: Season Finale Is Next Tuesday Handicap

prev_owners.owner: Just Wow Ltd, Mrs Melissa O'Neill & Robert Toogood

runners.comment: 9yo who is versatile trip-wise and was successful over 1m4f here last time; key player
runners.spotlight: All six of his British wins have been Beverley, including over 7.4f in July (good) and over 1m4f (good to firm) last month; up another 4lb but that was a comfortable success last time and he has won on soft ground in the past; key player.

runners.comment: Disappointing over hurdles last time and has something to prove after a break
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner (1m1f/1m2f) who suited by soft ground but last success was two years ago and she was disappointing over hurdles in June when last seen; on dangerous mark back in this sphere but she has something to prove after a break.

runners.comment: 3yo who justified favouritism at Newmarket and he's only 2lb higher here; respected
runners.spotlight: 3yo who got off the mark at the seventh attempt when justifying favouritism in Newmarket handicap (1m2f, good to firm) last month; that was a narrow success but he's only 2lb higher here and is open to more progress; all of his runs have been on good/good to firm but his dam won on soft.

quotes.race: JenningsBet Halstead Handicap
quotes.quote: Showyoutheropes was unlucky not to win at Newbury last time and it was nice for him to get some compensation here - Jackie Clover, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Runner-up at Epsom and Carlisle in last two starts and he's in the mix
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in February and he's finished runner-up five times since including at Epsom (8.5f) and Carlisle (1m1f, both on good) in last two starts; now switches to slow ground for the first time but has possibilities if he can continue the good work.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a very good race to finish second on his latest start at Haydock behind Adorla Of Achill. That was another great effort, and he has now won once and finished second three times for us.  He's come out of his last race very well and I'm going to look to run him on June 23 at Wolverhampton over an extended mile in a 0-70 for amateur jockeys. 18-06-25

runners.breeder: Whitsbury Manor Stud And Mrs M E Slade

prev_owners.owner: Mrs Linsey Pallas & Mrs Claire Ellison

runners.comment: Plenty of good efforts since March but his form has slumped in last two runs; new headgear
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who is versatile ground-wise; plenty of good efforts since returned to the Flat in March but his form has slumped in last two starts and losing run is now up 16; first-time blinkers replace visor.

stable_tour.quote: He's named after my grandson. I don't know why he didn't win on the Flat, but he's won twice over hurdles. He likes jumping and won at Catterick last time. He came back from that a bit sore but he's fine now and we're just waiting for better ground. He'll probably end up going back on the Flat and he should be winning in that sphere. 03-04-24

runners.comment: Struggled back on AW latest but she won three in a row on turf before that
runners.spotlight: Completed a 1m2f August hat-trick when shading a tight finish in apprentice event at Ripon under Rhys Elliott; failed to beat a rival back on AW last time but she couldn't get any cover in that slowly run race and could bounce back on this return to turf; all of her wins have been on good/good to firm but she's effective on good to soft.

quotes.race: William Hill Betting Done Properly Handicap
quotes.quote: We were unsure as Bay Dream Believer had a 5lb penalty and was only put up 2lb for her last win so it was a bit of a gamble but the prize money was good for the grade and she came out of the race really well. She's only a little pony but she tries hard - trainer Mark Walford.

stable_tour.quote: She's a very small mare but has one of the biggest hearts in training. We expect her to win again this summer - she's been fourth in her last two runs at Nottingham and Pontefract and is knocking on the door. She ran again at Pontefract on Monday, where the draw and a slow start made life very tricky for her. However, she finished powerfully to finish fourth behind Lord Warburton which was a great effort! 14-06-24

runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he's lost his way and needs a major revival
runners.spotlight: Returned from a break with a close second here (1m4f, good) in April but he's been disappointing since and was tailed off at Thirsk (reared at start) last time; now 3lb lower than for his last win but he needs a major revival.

runners.comment: Two wins and a close call before he stumbled and fell at Ripon last time; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and soft; won over C&D (dead-heat) and at Pontefract (1m2f, good to firm) this summer before a neck defeat at Carlisle (1m4f, good to firm) last month; had unfortunate experience when he stumbled and fell at Ripon last time but he still looks on a workable mark and is respected back at this track.

quotes.race: HEB Cleaning And Hygiene Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
quotes.quote: I'm really glad we got there, it would have been annoying after bumping off the rail. Star Start has got a tendency to go off quick and, being drawn wide, had I tried to make the running, I'd have done too much too soon. I had to sit and suffer, and it's paid off lovely - Charli Parker, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: A Ripon winner last year off a mark of 58, he has recently had a wind operation and won't be back until June. He's one for apprentices and amateurs, in particular our very own Charli Parker, who was on board at Ripon. He's versatile as regards trip andound. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Struggled in three qualifying runs but he could show a lot more now switched to handicap
runners.spotlight: Left Ralph Beckett for 32,000gns after one run; again well beaten in two AW races (1m) last month but he now goes handicapping and could show a lot more more on this step up to 1m2f; needs a close look for in-form yard.

prev_owners.owner: J Kay, T Goodair, A Skelton, C Johnson
Amo Racing Ltd, Mrs Doyle, Mr Morrin & PTN

runners.comment: Good fifth of 18 at Doncaster when last seen in March; needs watching in market on return
runners.spotlight: His last win was 17 months ago but he had some close calls at Newcastle during the winter and was a creditable fifth in big field at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) when last seen in March; remains well handicapped on old form and he needs watching in market on return.

runners.comment: Chased home an easy winner at Ripon last time but he's not won since 2021
runners.spotlight: Slipping down the weights and was runner-up behind an easy winner at Ripon (1m3f, good) last time; could run well again but this is a step back up in grade and he's not won since 2021.

quotes.race: Denford Stakes (Listed Race) (formerly The Washington Singer Stakes)
quotes.quote: Masekela has a couple of entries in the Champagne Stakes and the Royal Lodge and I need to talk to Andrew, but my view is that a mile is the right distance. William said he's a nice horse and there would be more to come. He said he'd make a nice 3yo, which we always felt as he's a big horse - Mick Mariscotti, owner.

stable_tour.quote: He won't run until later in the season, I gave him a good break when I got him from the sales last autumn. He'll be out from midsummer and I'm looking forward to him, we like him a lot. We bought him to run him at the big meetings and we gave him the winter off as he needed a break. He looks well handicapped and a mile and a half seems to be his trip. 23-04-24

runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo but well held in last two starts and has bit to prove for new yard
runners.spotlight: Started his handicap career with a promising third at Leicester (1m2f, good to soft) in May but he's not matched that form in two runs since; left Jane Chapple-Hyam for 12,000gns in July; still unexposed but has bit to prove on her stable debut.

runners.comment: Six-time C&D winner and has claims if she can recapture her spark back up in trip
runners.spotlight: Handles most ground and she recorded his sixth C&D win when scoring in July; disappointing in four runs since but latest was over an extended mile and she now goes back up in trip and down in grade; not ruled out off dangerous mark.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs P A Johnson & C H Stephenson

runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D on penultimate run but he didn't fire latest and is now 1-21
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of his last six starts including a close call over C&D (soft) in July; on same mark here but he didn't fire at Ayr last time and that took his record to 1-21; tongue-tie worn last time is removed.

quotes.race: Lake District Handicap (Jockey Club Grassroots Middle Distance Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Ryan (jockey) did a great job and it's just a horse that's taken us a little while to figure him out. We started Whatacracker over seven because he shows lots of speed at home and we've gradually stepped him up. We thought he'd win at Newmarket last time and he disappointed us a bit but what we see at home is what we've seen on the track today - Mark Walford, trainer.

racecards.verdict: Top of the list is DR RIO (nap), who has won here in two of his last four starts including a comfortable success over 1m4f last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Into Combat, 9/2 Dr Rio, 5/1 Sonic Pioneer, 8/1 Star Start,  Masekela, 10/1 Bay Dream Believer, 14/1 Freddy Robinson,  San Munoz, 16/1 Powerful Response,  Dandy's Angel,  Whatacracker, 25/1 Red Trail,  Prince Achille

racecards.race_name: Borders Carers Centre Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Stable won this last year and this gelding's Bellewstown win sets the standard
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who got off the mark at the third attempt over hurdles at Bellewstown (2m4f, good) three weeks ago, despite some indifferent jumping; posted an RPR of 127 in the process and that sets the standard here; stable won this last year; softening ground might not be ideal (withdrawn from a yielding to soft engagement).

runners.comment: Trained by Mark Walford when tailed off in a Wetherby bumper in early 2024
runners.spotlight: Trained by Mark Walford when tailed off in a soft-ground Wetherby bumper and that was in March 2024; would need to see some confidence behind him.

runners.comment: Dual bumper winner; hurdle form leaves him vulnerable against Jackson Lamb
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner on good ground and encountered the same conditions for his two placed efforts over hurdles (2m/2m4f); RPRs leave with a fair bit to find with Jackson Lamb, though the addition of cheekpieces could help his jumping which hasn't been foot perfect; he was taken out of a soft-ground engagement.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a good race when second at Hexham last month. He's still quite keen and I thought he'd put the race to bed but he got collared close home. He's progressing nicely and just needs a bit of confidence with his jumping. He's a big, scopey horse who could go up in trip again. He was given a mark of 106 and that opens up options for him, and we'll look to get his head in front over hurdles. 10-09-25

runners.comment: Out of a Galileo mare but this is a very belated debut at the age of seven
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; dam well held 1m-2m, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Sir Parky; this is a very belated debut at the age of seven.

runners.comment: Bought for £42,000 soon after winning a good-ground 3m Irish point in April
runners.spotlight: Bought for £42,000 soon after winning a good-ground 3m Irish point in April; has joined a respected yard for rules action; best to let the betting guide.

racecards.verdict: The chances are that JACKSON LAMB will follow up his Bellewstown success for last season's successful stable.
racecards.betting_forecast: 10/11 Jackson Lamb, 5/2 Jentobello, 11/4 Magic Gloves, 33/1 Avalon Beach, 66/1 Top Of The Stars

racecards.race_name: Paul Ferguson's Jumpers To Follow Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Off five months since a creditable Perth sixth; he needs to hit the ground running
runners.spotlight: A dual hurdles scorer (around 2m4f) in 2023-24 season but very lightly raced since and off for five months since a creditable sixth in 2m4f Perth handicap; he needs to hit the ground running.

quotes.race: bet365 Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Serious Operator is improving, his jumping was perfect. We were a bit worried about the ground. His hurdling is outstanding and he'll go chasing next season - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

quotes.race: Bet Boost At bet365 Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: When Serious Operator gets his ground and gets everything sorted he's a heck of a horse. He couldn't get out of the heavy ground last time. Today he travelled and I probably got there too soon. I hope he's turned the corner now - Patrick Wadge, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He'll probably have one more run over hurdles, maybe at Cheltenham on Friday, and then it will be chasing for him after that. We've wanted to go chasing but there wasn't an obvious target at the start of the season and he's not slow. It was a good performance at Ayr in April, so we'll give him one more chance over hurdles to see if he can improve again. 15-11-23

He came to us from Daniel Murphy in Ireland having won two point-to-points. He won first time out for us under rules at Kelso and, after an unhappy run at Cheltenham in October, returned this month with a win over 2m4½f at Newcastle. He's not over-big but has a nice engine and stays exceedingly well. He's better on good to soft. After his Cheltenham run Tom [Scudamore] suggested he had breathing issues and he was subsequently operated on. He won after the operation but genuinely seems better than ever and we could head back to Cheltenham this weekend. He's a strong galloper and jumps well and long term I can't wait to see him novice chasing. 25-01-23

runners.comment: Career-best Perth win latest; not taken lightly if his stamina holds out over longer trip
runners.spotlight: Resumed from four months off with a career-best victory in 2m Perth handicap hurdle 32 days ago; up another 4lb but not taken lightly if his stamina holds out over this longer trip.

quotes.race: Howden Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: In a good race like that they were going too quick. In fairness to Lee, he took his time and got Here Comes Georgie into it lovely. He did it well - Patrick Neville, trainer.

quotes.race: Follow @racingtv On X Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Here Comes Georgie had a stone bruise the first time he ran over fences at Hexham, he wasn't right. He was always going to be nicer over fences - Paddy Neville, trainer.

quotes.race: Stellantis At Auto Services Perth Ltd. Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Here Comes Georgie is such a sound horse who likes the track, and we sure like it here too! We love coming up here. It was mighty as we were just touched off in the first. We know he likes the ground, but we might have a go over fences with him - Patrick Neville, trainer.

quotes.race: Scone Estates Stone Of Destiny Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I was riding out for Paddy all last season, and the harder we tried, the worse results got.  The move is already repaying itself, and we've got lots of well-handicapped horses in the short term.  HERE COMES GEORGIES's best form speaks for itself, like beating Cracking Rhapsody, but a few things haven't happened for him since - Henry Brooke, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: He's been a great servant to us as he's won three races this season already. He won for the first time over fences at Wetherby last month and seems to be on the up. He was pulled up on his chase debut in May but he had a stone bruise, so we put a line through that. He went back over hurdles, winning twice at Perth and running well in a really hot race at Cheltenham. We schooled him over fences and he was brilliant. He was always going to be a chaser and seems to be a nice two-miler who could pick up more races. There's a race for him at Ascot on December 21 as long as the ground is right. After that, he could be one to take to the Dublin Racing Festival in February. There were only a couple runners in the novice chase there last year, for a good pot of money, and Leopardstown is always good ground. That would be his aim for the season. 11-12-24

runners.comment: Good second at Newton Abbot 18 days ago; must enter calculations despite a 2lb rise
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Taunton (2m) in February and in good form since, visored when runner-up in 2m5f handicap at Newton Abbot 18 days ago; he must enter calculations despite a 2lb weights rise with the headgear again sported.

quotes.race: MansionBet Beaten By A Head Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Luttrell Lad is nice. He had very good bumper form and it was nice to get him started off over hurdles. He can be a bit fizzy but we went a good gallop there and it allowed him to settle and jump well. Day one is out of the way now and there's plenty to look forward to - Tom O'Brien, jockey.

quotes.race: Pertemps Network Standard Open National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Luttrell Lad was very green at Stratford first time. He learned a lot and he kept on well here - Richard Johnson, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's gone for a winter break and will be back in the spring but he's a good horse for us. We got him handicapped on the Flat [75] and his last run was probably just one too many. 08-11-22

He's been a decent horse for us, winning over hurdles as well as running a cracker when eighth in the County Hurdle at the festival in March. I was happy enough with his reappearance effort at Haydock last month and then he looked like he was going to win at Hereford on Saturday but got caught late on having started racing way too soon. Unfortunately he's been plagued with problems; he's got a suppressed immune response without a shadow of a doubt and has been very difficult to train. As a result he'll never achieve what he possibly could have done. He's incredibly genuine and a lot of horses wouldn't have faced the bridle suffering what he's been through. He's had to have constant treatment and the owners have had to be so patient - for them it's like owning two horses in training as he has to have constant physio. I'd still like to think he has a nice handicap in him, but he's a mystery. I don't know if he'd stay as far as 3m. When we first got him we thought he'd be a real speed merchant who'd want top of the ground, but he's not as fast as he thinks he is. 20-12-23

prev_owners.owner: McNeill Family and Prodec Networks Ltd

runners.comment: Beat only one on yard debut at Perth (2m4f) last month; he has something to prove now
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Olly Murphy with victory in 2m4f Wetherby handicap hurdle in March but he beat only one on his yard debut at Perth (2m4f) last month; he has something to prove now.

quotes.race: Thank You & Good Luck Charlo' Selling Hurdle
quotes.quote: That's great, Tamar Bridge has been a good servant for his owner. He'll have to go back to handicaps now - Olly Murphy, trainer.

quotes.race: Unibet - 2021 Horserace Betting Operator Of The Year Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: It was tough for Tamar Bridge first time against seasoned handicappers, but he's an out-and-out stayer. He's a good horse and will make a lovely chaser in time - Olly Murphy, trainer.

quotes.race: Play New Slots Weekly At Betfair Casino "Newcomers" Standard Open National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Tamar Bridge is a big, fine horse whose future lies over further. He loved that ground and showed a good attitude - Aidan Coleman, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He left his season behind after a hard race when he won at Aintree last December. The plan is to go chasing now and he's freshened up, jumps a fence well and is hopefully going to make up into a really nice staying handicap chaser. He's ready to start and will go as soon as the rain comes as he'll need the soft ground. 27-10-22

He won his first two races this season, at Wetherby and Aintree, and in retrospect I was wrong to send him to Cheltenham in search of his hat-trick. He is on a mark only 6lb higher than his last success so I hope he can win again this season. He has the makings of a lovely chaser next season as he looks the part. 26-01-22

runners.comment: Irish challenger posted a good Downpatrick second latest; up 4lb but very much considered
runners.spotlight: Irish challenger is on a long losing run but she posted a good second of nine in 2m5f Downpatrick handicap hurdle 23 days ago; up 4lb but very much one to consider.

quotes.race: Bluegrass Horse Feeds Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: Rockview Consort  jumped well. John (McConnell) does a good job schooling his horses at home, he doesn't like to miss with these nice ones first-time out. I kept it simple, it was a nice even gallop and you can do that around here on good ground. It was a pretty good performance and she's one we'll look forward to - Ben Harvey, jockey, talking to Racing TV

quotes.race: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Maiden
quotes.quote: Rockview Consort probably wants further but we said we would have another crack at this trip. It is great to get the win and she will go on turf as well - John McConnell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She took a while to win her maiden but did so nicely at Dundalk last time over 1m2½f. She has a 3lb higher rating on the turf and, having given her a break after that success, she'll be brought back for 1m4f handicaps on the grass. She also jumps well and is a possible for some hurdling this summer. 06-04-23

runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; only 11th at Cartmel in July but could bounce back here after a break
runners.spotlight: A three-time scorer last season, including twice over C&D; came in only 11th in 2m6f Cartmel handicap in July but no surprise to see her bounce back here after a break.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Go North Cab On Target Handicap Hurdle (Series Final) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I love racing because of the horses and Starlyte is the sort of horse I love. She's only tiny but she's so brave. You have to drop her in and let her pass horses and I'm delighted for the owners Allan and Anne Grant who've just got married - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

quotes.race: Follow Thehawkponteland On Instagram Mares' Handicap Hurdle (Go North Lady Buttons Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Starlyte needs a fast-run two-and-a-half miles and she's the most game horse. It's her attitude that wins her races - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She came to us from the Flat and made a flying start by winning a 2m4½f Newcastle handicap hurdle. We'll keep her to that trip with a view to going up to 3m later. She desperately needs good ground and is ready to go as soon as things dry out. 25-01-23

runners.comment: A ready winner for his new yard at Hexham three weeks ago; up 6lb but he's a player
runners.spotlight: Running well without winning in Ireland for Thomas Coyle and he made a winning start for his new handler in 2m4f handicap hurdle at Hexham three weeks ago; up 6lb but he was well on top at the finish there so is a player.

runners.comment: Good third at Sedgefield 13 days ago; this C&D scorer can't be discounted
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Cartmel (2m1f) in August and followed it up with a good third of nine in 2m4f handicap at Sedgefield 13 days ago; this C&D scorer can't be discounted.

quotes.race: Go North Sea Pigeon Series Final (A Handicap Hurdle) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: The Navigator fell in this race last year and he never really recovered. I deliberately went to Sedgefield because I thought if there was anywhere he could get his confidence back it would be there, and he needed it. He's got a lot of ability, he's a big beautiful horse, he's stunning - Dianne Sayer, trainer.

racecards.verdict: There was plenty to like about the breakthrough win of Tristan Davidson's new recruit DIVILABOTHER at Hexham so he looks the way to go
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Rockview Consort, 3/1 Here Comes Georgie, 100/30 Luttrell Lad, 9/2 Divilabother, 10/1 Serious Operator,  The Navigator, 14/1 Starlyte, 50/1 Tamar Bridge

racecards.race_name: RBC Brewin Dolphin Handicap Chase (Go North Monet's Garden Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Captured this prize a year ago; it would come as no surprise to see him play a major role
runners.spotlight: Captured this prize a year ago and in good form until ridden aggressively when tenth in 2m5f Cartmel handicap chase 23 days ago; it would come as no surprise to see him play a major role here.

quotes.race: Ashleybank Investments Reg And Betty Tweedie Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Half Shot is a good old yardstick who keeps coming back and he wins his couple every year. When the second horse came to him he battled away well - Brian Hughes, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: James And Gloria Roche Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Half Shot was weighted to carry 8st 5lb at Newcastle (in the Rehearsal, with Shishkin compressing the weights), so we thought we'd come here. He won that well and jumped class there. I thought it was a great ride from Brian Hughes. I think this horse has got a good race in him, he's very consistent and genuine. He'll get a hike for that, so we'll have to look at better races. He ran in the Scottish National last season, so we could look at that again in the spring - Iain Jardine, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran at Cartmel last time but didn't get his own way. There are a couple of meetings at Kelso coming up and he's being targeted there on September 17. He'll probably race until November as he doesn't want winter ground. He's coming up to 12, so we'll see if he's still got the enthusiasm, but he's got another few days lined up. 10-09-25

runners.comment: C&D scorer; he has won when fresh too so enters calculations on his return
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer; not seen since a solid fifth of nine in 2m4f handicap here in April but has won when fresh so enters calculations on his return.

stable_tour.quote: He was a pleasing second at Ayr on his hurdles debut, beaten a neck by the Gordon Elliott-trained Nonbinding, and on that run he looks sure to win a novice hurdle in the north. He's probably a horse for two or two and a half miles. 09-11-22

prev_owners.owner: Roderick Ryan & Thomas B Breen & M D Minch

runners.comment: Showed little in two starts for current yard in the spring; hard to make a case for
runners.spotlight: A very useful ex-Irish chaser at his best but he's been off the track since showing little in two starts here and at Sedgefield for his current yard in the spring; he's hard to make a case for after 159 days off.

quotes.race: Vinegar Hill Beginners Chase
quotes.quote: He's honest anyway because, for a whole circuit, he didn't get a look at a jump but he kept jumping through. The loose horse went to run Dot Love's horse [The Mulcare Rover] at one stage but he jumped through again. The fences were the easy part - it was navigating the loose horses in between which was the hard bit - Paul Townend, rider

quotes.race: Welcome To Wexford Racecourse Rated Novice Hurdle
quotes.quote: He enjoyed the trip and seems to be improving all of the time. Hopefully there is a race for him at one of the spring Festivals - Willie is well able to find them. Hopefully he can win again - Danny Mullins, rider

stable_tour.quote: He goes for the 2m4f handicap chase at Punchestown next Saturday. 09-11-20

runners.comment: In very good order, third at Cartmel last time; very much one to consider off 1lb lower
runners.spotlight: Won on first two chasing starts last autumn and he arrives in very good order, a strong-travelling third of 11 in 2m5f handicap at Cartmel last time; he's very much one to consider off a 1lb lower mark.

quotes.race: Racing To School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Nickelforce is such a good jumper. With amateur races it's tricky but Charlie [Case, jockey] knew the horse, so that was an added bonus. He's something to look forward to - Linda Lovell, part-owner.

runners.comment: Fair third of five in 3m1f handicap chase at Cartmel in July; he's one for the shortlist
runners.spotlight: Veteran course scorer who was a very respectable third of five in 3m1f handicap chase at Cartmel in July; he's one for the shortlist.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a good race when third at Southwell early this month. He blew up a bit after aeak and it wouldn't be his track either. Hopefully he might go a couple of places better next time. He's still a novice but has a good bit of experience and should win his share of races. 25-10-23

runners.comment: Off since March but has run well off a break so can't be discounted; visor goes back on
runners.spotlight: Off since recording a good second in 2m4f Ayr handicap chase in March but he has run well off a break so can't be discounted; visor goes back on too.

stable_tour.quote: He had wind surgery after winning a point-to-point last spring. It clearly had the desired effect as he won on his rules debut at Ayr in November when taking a 2m4½f maiden hurdle. He finished second on his only other outing back at Ayr last month but that form looks quite decent. He's a nice horse to look forward to as he progresses. 25-01-23

prev_owners.owner: Middleham Park Racing XLVIII & Peter Lamb
Lord Daresbury, Ecurie Mme Jacques Cypres & Laurent Couetil

runners.comment: Second run back from long absence when pulled up at Worcester 17 days ago; bit to prove
runners.spotlight: Scored over fences at Worcester (2m4f) in September, 2023; made an encouraging return from 22 months off when fourth at Uttoxeter in July but pulled up in 2m4f Worcester handicap chase 17 days ago; has something to prove.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Glajou is definitely improving and is such a big four-year-old. He's getting stronger and better and learning how to race. He could make into a nice horse. It was a solid race at Chepstow last time and he's going the right way.  Harry Cobden, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He won over fences last year but is still qualified for novice hurdles and will go that route. He was slightly frustrating last season and didn't always find that much off the bridle but he's still only five and I hope he will finish his races better now he's another year stronger. 08-11-21

runners.comment: Not disgraced when third in handicap chase at Cartmel 25 days ago; he's no forlorn hope
runners.spotlight: Veteran is winless since 2023 but he wasn't disgraced when third of six in 2m1f handicap chase at Cartmel 25 days ago; he's no forlorn hope.

stable_tour.quote: A horse with a great following at our local tracks. You can set your watch by him and come the spring, he should be able to add to his three wins. We've tried him up in grade and he gets found out, but the handicapper is dropping him a little bit now and that should make life easier. He jumps well and is a proper trier. 24-01-24

prev_owners.owner: Mrs Louise Whillans & King for a Day
Mrs Louise Whillans & King for a Day

runners.comment: Not discredited when third at Hexham three weeks ago; possibilities
runners.spotlight: Gained a first victory since 2023 at Hexham (2m4f) in June and not discredited when third of seven in similar event there three weeks ago; possibilities.

quotes.race: Prince Philip Perpetual Challenge Trophy Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I thought Cracking Destiny would run well but I didn't think he had that in him. Craig said they went quick early on and it played to his strengths, as he sits off the pace and needs them to go fast over two miles - Ewan Whillans, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

quotes.race: Healthy Pets Chase (A Novices' Limited Handicap)
quotes.quote: Cracking Destiny's come to life in handicaps and jumps fences really well. He's a horse we'll try to win a couple more with and will probably go to a dispersal sale at the end of the season, but that's because the owners want top, top horses and Gold Cup winners. Cracking Destiny won't be that, but he'll do a good job and will have a great time until May - Ryan Tongue, racing manager to Mike Grech and Stuart Pakin, owners.

runners.comment: Shaped well when runner-up over fences at Uttoxeter and Worcester; weighted to go close
runners.spotlight: Signed off over hurdles with victory at Market Rasen (2m2f) and he has shaped well switched to chasing when runner-up in 2m4f handicaps at Uttoxeter and Worcester (winner has gone in again since) this summer; tongue tied now and visor replaces cheekpieces; weighted to go close.

stable_tour.quote: This is an exciting horse we've acquired. He's a half-brother to Ben Pauling's staying chaser Twig and won his point-to-point at Larkhill earlier this month, showing a good turn of foot to score impressively. He'll be one to follow when he starts under rules. 20-12-23

racecards.verdict: Tom Lacey's LET'S SAIL AWAY can race off the same mark as when a very good recent second behind a next-time-out winner at Worcester.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Let's Sail Away, 9/2 Cracking Destiny,  Nickelforce, 7/1 Step Above,  High Moon, 8/1 No Regrets, 9/1 Half Shot, 14/1 Chapel Green, 16/1 Glajou, 66/1 Pont Aven

racecards.race_name: Abbey Fine Wines Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.comment: Generally reliable 7yo who was a rallying fourth at Cartmel last time; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who has reached the frame in five of his last seven runs including when a rallying fourth at Cartmel (2m1f, soft) in July; this step back up in trip looks a positive and he should go well again after a short break.

runners.comment: 1-21 over hurdles but she ran well at Hexham latest and could make a bold bid back in trip
runners.spotlight: Hard to predict and is 1-21 over hurdles but she went close at Musselburgh in March and was a front-running fourth of 15 at Hexham (2m4f, good) three weeks ago; should be fine on this drop back in trip and it wouldn't be a surprise if she made a bold bid.

quotes.race: Paddy Madine Tareesh Memorial Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Ian has a good job done schooling with O'Faolains Glory and she had been round a couple of point-to-point tracks and that. We knew that's the way to ride her, go hard. She pulled herself up a bit, but thankfully we held on - Keith Donoghue, jockey.

runners.comment: Got back on the scoresheet with dominant display at Cartmel; big player again up 7lb
runners.spotlight: Recorded his seventh hurdle win when making all off reduced mark at Cartmel (2m1f, good) last month; back up 7lb but that was a dominant display under Joshua Thompson and he remains well treated on old form; big player.

quotes.race: Brit Direct Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
quotes.quote: Ambassador came to us in good form and all we've had to do is tick him over. He was given a good ride by Brian Hughes and it just sort of fell right for us really - Micky Hammond, trainer.

runners.breeder: Howard Barton Stud & Newsells Park Stud

prev_owners.owner: John F Doyle & Dee L'Estrange & Weld-Spec (Glasgow) Ltd

runners.comment: Fair fourth back over hurdles at Hexham but he's now 0-19 and is hard to predict
runners.spotlight: Switched back from chasing to hurdling with a respectable fourth at Hexham (2m, good) three weeks ago but that took his record to 0-19 and he's not easy to predict; others preferred.

runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo but she struggled on handicap debut at Perth and has something to prove
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo who was runner-up in a novice here (2m, good) in May but she's struggled in both runs since including on handicap debut at Perth (3m, good; 20-1) last month; now takes a big drop back in trip but she didn't jump well last time and has something to prove.

runners.comment: Overall record of 0-14 and has form figures of P8P7P over fences and hurdles this year
runners.spotlight: Well treated on some runner-up efforts in handicaps last summer but he has form figures of P8P7P over fences and hurdles this year; overall record is now 0-14 and he needs a big turnaround.

runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles and he struggled in final two runs last year; opposable on return
runners.spotlight: On much reduced mark but he's still a maiden after 13 starts over hurdles and he struggled in his final two runs last year; opposable back from 293 days off.

runners.comment: Went close over C&D in May and he's been placed in last three starts; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Chase winner who is 0-9 over hurdles but he went close over C&D in May and has been placed at Bangor (2m) and Stratford (2m2f) in his last three starts; usually races prominently and has each-way claims again back up in trip.

runners.breeder: R D And Mrs J S Chugg
runners.owner: D Mullen, G Barker And Partners
runners.comment: Belatedly off the mark at Hexham but he's not gone on from that; opposable
runners.spotlight: 8yo who has form at up to 3m1f; belatedly off the mark over hurdles at Hexham (2m, good) in May but he's not gone on from that and was tailed off at Cartmel (2m6f, good to soft) when last seen in June; hurdling record is now 1-34 and he looks opposable.

runners.comment: 0-8 and he's struggled in his four handicaps; needs improvement in cheekpieces
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has struggled in his four handicaps (2m-2m7f) and was well held in a Market Rasen novice last time; cheekpieces are now added and he needs improvement back up in trip.

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late W G Young

runners.comment: Dual course winner but he's struggled back under rules in his last three starts
runners.spotlight: Won twice here (including over C&D) in 2023 but this year's pointing form is uninspiring and he has struggled back over fences and hurdles in last three starts; mark is tumbling but he has plenty to prove.

racecards.verdict: Top of the list is AMBASSADOR (nap) who cashed in on a reduced mark with a dominant display under Joshua Thompson at Cartmel last month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 15/8 Ambassador, 4/1 Guillaume, 5/1 Heart Above, 6/1 O'Faolains Glory, 8/1 Nathan Wells, 12/1 Woody's Boy, 16/1 Green Bonnet,  Blended Stealth, 25/1 Ascension Day, 33/1 The Geordie Ginge, 40/1 Darkest Day

racecards.race_name: Abbey Fine Wines Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.comment: Off since pulled up over C&D in Apri; handily weighted though if back on her A-game
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Hexham (2m) last April but she's been off since pulled up over C&D in Apri; handily weighted though if back on her A-game here.

quotes.race: Tay Valley Chasers Patrick Crofts Memorial Junior "National Hunt" Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: She's tough, she's very very tough. She's Notjoeking isn't very big but she's got a heart. She had a look coming down to the last which didn't help me. If she'd maybe galloped down to that  I think she'd have won a bit more comfortably - Patrick Wadge, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Not disgraced after two months off when ninth at Market Rasen; very much one to consider
runners.spotlight: Bagged a pair of 2m handicaps at Market Rasen in June and not disgraced after two months off when ninth over 2m3f there last time; very much one to consider off a 2lb lower mark.

runners.comment: Last at Hereford 11 months ago on final run for Tim Vaughan; lots more needed for new yard
runners.spotlight: Opened her account at Fontwell (2m5f) last September but came in last of nine at Hereford (2m4f) 11 months ago on her final run for Tim Vaughan; since changed hands for £1,000 and she needs to hit the ground running for her new stable.

stable_tour.quote: She was unlucky at Hereford two starts ago as she would have won easily but for falling at the last. That was more than a year ago and she returned in the autumn, finishing fourth, but she doesn't care for winter ground and the owners have been very patient. She'll be out soon and will be nice and fresh. She has plenty of ability and should go close. 20-03-24

runners.comment: Still to get his head in front and he comes here on the back of a below-par Cartmel sixth
runners.spotlight: Still to get his head in front and he comes here on the back of a below-par sixth in 2m1f Cartmel handicap hurdle 25 days ago; others appeal more.

prev_owners.owner: Exors of the Late Nicholas Kelly

runners.comment: In very good form, third at Uttoxeter in July; a likely player after a break
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough success at Sedgefield (2m1f) in April and has continued in very good form, third of 12 in 2m Uttoxeter handicap in July; cheekpieces replace blinkers and she's a likely player after a break.

runners.comment: Offered little for new yard back at Hexham three weeks ago; he's hard to make a case for
runners.spotlight: Only poor form shown on the Flat for Jacqueline Coward in 2024 and he offered little for his new yard back hurdling after 11 months off at Hexham (2m4f) three weeks ago; he's hard to make a case for.

quotes.race: Pertemps Network Handicap
quotes.quote: Oasis Prince is a lovely horse who has got better and better since his first run. It was quite tough out there with the headwind - Franny Norton, rider.

runners.comment: Maiden hurdler who comes here on the back of two poor efforts on the Flat last month
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler who comes here on the back of two poor efforts on the Flat last month; has something to prove.

runners.comment: Good second in Hexham chase three weeks ago; needs to back it up back hurdling
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when runner-up in 2m4f Hexham handicap chase three weeks ago; he needs to back it up now returned to this sphere.

stable_tour.quote: He's had four runs since we purchased him in Ireland and he's been third, third, second and second - including last time out at Hexham behind Halpha Soleil. He looks certain to be getting his head in front soon as the first three at Hexham came a long way clear - he just bumped into one there. 14-06-24

runners.comment: 0-21 and was a below-par sixth in Bangor handicap hurdle 12 days ago; others are preferred
runners.spotlight: 0-21 and he turned in a below-par sixth of nine in 2m Bangor handicap hurdle 12 days ago; others are preferred.

runners.comment: A good runner-up at Perth (2m4f) in May but he's shown little in three runs since
runners.spotlight: A good runner-up at Perth (2m4f) in May but he's shown little in three runs since, pulled up back there (2m4f) nine days ago; it's easy to look elsewhere.

stable_tour.quote: He was really disappointing at Nottingham last time out which was not like him. He hated the quick ground and together with the trip of 1m2f it was all against him. He had to work very hard to get the lead from a wide draw and then fell in a heap. Ignore that form as back on a straight track over a mile should bring out the best in him. 21-04-21

racecards.verdict: An uncompetitive handicap and this looks a very good opportunity for Micky Hammond's reliable MILAJESS to bag a deserved second success
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Huit Reflets, 4/1 Milajess, 5/1 She's Notjoeking, 11/2 Balally Park, 6/1 Game Beaaa, 10/1 Clan Chieftain, 14/1 Mr Globalist, 16/1 Oasis Prince, 25/1 State Of Honor, 50/1 Ivynator

racecards.race_name: Belhaven Brewery Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Consistent gelding and won this 12 months ago on his reappearance when 4lb lower
runners.spotlight: Won this 12 months ago on his reappearance when 4lb lower and then finished a 2l second to Primoz at Wetherby, the form of which could be reversed on these more favourable terms; consistent and should have every chance.

runners.comment: Inconsistent since returning from a layoff and beaten 50l and 18l in his last two races
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent since returning from a layoff earlier this year and beaten 50l and 18l in his last two races; others preferred.

quotes.race: Make Your Home A Happy Haus Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Blinkers have been the making of Fringill Dike. For some reason over hurdles he just lost his confidence a little bit, he didn't like being crowded. The instant we put blinkers on, he was just a different horse. Life is going to get tougher now as his mark is getting towards 130 and the better jumpers will be coming out before too long. There's a race at the first meeting at Wetherby that may suit as long as the ground hasn't gone too slow by then - Jedd O'Keefe, trainer.

runners.comment: Last season went well until his struggles at Cheltenham/Aintree; had wind surgery
runners.spotlight: Beat Pay The Piper on his chase debut and comeback run last October, and four runs later won a match over C&D (soft); lost his race at the start when only 6-1 for the Grand Annual and has undergone wind surgery since again performing below market expectations at Aintree in April; retains pleny of potential as a chaser.

quotes.race: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Primoz is one of the most talented horses we've dealt with and we've changed his training programme a bit. I don't know where he'll go next but he's a very talented horse if we can get some consistency into him - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

quotes.race: Mary Lofthouse Supporting Spinal Research Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: I got worried because I'd said Primoz is a lovely horse, he's been a great hurdler and is going to make a better chaser, then the chips are down and he's got to prove it. But I think he is going to be a better chaser. I loved the way he jumped - and I believe El Elefante did the same at Worcester, so happy days! Pay The Piper is a real decent two-miler and Primoz was pretty good today - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

quotes.race: Remus Uomo Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: I'm delighted with Primoz. He won well first time then he's been in the wilderness but it's the ground, he doesn't like it soft. He's got a lot of ability and we just needed to get him tighter at home. He looked great today and he'll go chasing next season - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's such a lovely horse. He won twice over hurdles last season and his jumping was fantastic when he won on his chase debut at Wetherby this month. We'll take him to some of the bigger tracks now and he should stay as his breeding suggests he'll do so, so we'll probably step him up in trip sooner rather than later. He needs good ground. 30-10-24

runners.breeder: Mrs J A Niven & Mrs D M Ewart
runners.owner: Clova Syndicate And P D Niven

prev_owners.owner: Clova Syndicate And Mrs J A Niven

runners.comment: Hasn't been running badly in handicaps at about this trip but looks vulnerable
runners.spotlight: An 11yo now who isn't as good as he was and has predictably lost some of his old speed; hasn't been running badly in handicaps at about this trip but looks vulnerable.

quotes.race: Scotty Brand Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I couldn't explain Cheltenham. Malystic travelled well and he just never picked up, it may have been the ground. Apart from that he's been grand and that's the third big handicap he's won this season. That will be it for this season - Peter Niven, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Extra Places Every Day Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
