quotes.quote: That was a good performance, particularly with the way Malystic travelled - he was absolutely swinging turning in. He basically missed last season after picking up a terrible infection, so he has low mileage. He's a proper horse, but things haven't gone his way. The only conditions race I was considering was the Game Spirit but it might come a bit quick and then there's the Red Rum at Aintree. I'd love to have a go at the Celebration Chase at the end of the season, but we'll see - Peter Niven, trainer.

quotes.race: William Hill Castleford Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Malystic had a hiccup at Cheltenham and he scoped badly afterwards but we got him cleared out and he was in great form. He'd run well first time and this was the right race for him. I hope he's a nice horse - Peter Niven, trainer.

quotes.race: Vertem Investing For The Future Standard Open National Hunt Flat Race (All-Weather)
quotes.quote: Malystic is a nephew of [handicap and Graded winning hurdler] Clever Cookie and is the most like him we've had. He wouldn't have his turn of foot but he's a lovely horse. We'll finish him now or maybe go to Aintree and then he'll go jumping next season - Peter Niven, trainer.

runners.comment: An 11yo now and last time was well held at Cartmel where he usually goes well
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner from 2m1f to 2m5f; he is getting long in the tooth now and last time was well held at Cartmel where he usually goes well.

quotes.race: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Arthur's Quay showed a bit of class there and I was really pleased with him. Richie (McLernon) felt it might be worth having a crack at the Topham in the spring with him - there was lots going on in that race but he handled it all well and his jumping came to the fore - Ben Haslam, trainer.

quotes.race: John J. Galvin Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: He's a lovely horse. We expected him to run well and it was grand that he won. Mark [Walsh] gave him a lovely ride. The step up to two and half miles did the job. We know the family well. His half-brother [The Big Apple] isn't a bad horse - I won a bumper on him so he mustn't have been bad! He's a nice horse that we've always liked from day one - Padraig Roche, trainer of Arthur's Quay

runners.comment: Fair shout on his close third 12 months ago and has been in competitive form at Perth
runners.spotlight: Appeared to have the race in the bag at Perth (2m4f, good) last month only to idle late on and get nabbed close home; ridden more patiently back there last week and couldn't pick up (beaten about 6l); however he was a close third to Pay The Piper in this last year and has a fair shout with visor replacing cheekpieces (worn last three races).

runners.owner: The North West National Hunt Racing Club
runners.comment: Winning hurdler last time and a major player if taking to fences at the first attempt
runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner who has been in good form over hurdles this summer, winning at Uttoxeter (2m, good) two months ago; this 5yo is a major player if taking to fences at the first attempt.

racecards.verdict: The interesting horse is PRIMOZ for whom things didn't pan out too favourably in the big 2m handicaps at Cheltenham and Aintree.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Primoz, 3/1 Pay The Piper, 4/1 Dog Fox, 5/1 Garde Des Champs, 8/1 Malystic, 10/1 Fringill Dike, 16/1 Arthur's Quay

racecards.race_name: racingtv.com/freetrial Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: In second when slipping up after the last fence in a 3m soft-ground point
runners.spotlight: Beaten 17l in a point bumper but was in second when slipping up after the last fence in a 3m soft-ground point at Friars Haugh in February; clearly has some ability.

runners.comment: Prominent throughout when third of 14 on good ground at Wexford in July
runners.spotlight: Prominent throughout when third of 14 on good ground at Wexford in July; was beaten about 8l in the end but should have a future for new yard.

runners.owner: M McHale J Munroe D Naisbitt & S Hodges
runners.comment: Flat-bred newcomer from a yard that's 1-21 in bumpers the last five seasons
runners.spotlight: Flat bred and cost 45,000gns as a yearling; closely related to 1m2f/1m4f winner Ensured (RPR 98); stable 1-21 in bumpers the last five seasons.

runners.owner: Castle View Racing & W F Corbett
runners.comment: Out of a bumper winner and was close up at Hexham (good to soft) despite racing freely
runners.spotlight: Out of a bumper winner and was close up at Hexham (good to soft) despite racing freely; considered.

runners.comment: Weak in the betting when chasing home two Irish raiders at Perth but wasn't beaten far
runners.spotlight: Weak in the betting when chasing home two Irish raiders at Perth (good) but wasn't beaten far; did race freely under today's 10lb claimer but she should be wiser this time.

runners.comment: A positive jumping pedigree but a rare bumper runner for the stable
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam unraced half-sister to smart 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Mr Thriller and useful winners Gaspara (2m-2m3f hurdle) and Larry (2m3f-3m hurdle/chase); positive pedigree but a rare bumper runner for the stable.

runners.comment: 80-1 when tailed off on good ground at Hexham three weeks ago; no real appeal
runners.spotlight: 80-1 when tailed off on good ground at Hexham three weeks ago; no real appeal.

runners.breeder: Shade Oak Stud, M Fleming & P Stephen
runners.comment: £1,000 3yo; ninth foal; half-sister to Rapid Response (useful 2m5f/3m1f chase winner)
runners.spotlight: £1,000 3yo; ninth foal; half-sister to Rapid Response (useful 2m5f/3m1f chase winner), Malindi Bay (point/bumper) and Lock Down Luke (bumper and 2m/2m6f hurdle); dam 12.5f Flat/2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner; yard 0-9 in bumpers in recent seasons.

racecards.verdict: Gijima ran well at Perth but  NOBOTHERATALL might not have to improve all that much on his third at Wexford.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Gijima, 2/1 Nobotheratall, 7/2 Dotties Promise, 7/1 Monte A Bord, 16/1 Blue Indigo, 33/1 Nufinbutdatruth,  La Grande Keely, 66/1 Strathcatcha

racecards.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap

runners.comment: 2nd in both C&D starts; well suited by slow ground; laboured effort at Brighton on Monday
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in both C&D starts; good record on slow ground and he finished second at Epsom (6f, good) last month; slow starts are a recurring theme though and he turned in a laboured effort at Brighton on Monday.

quotes.race: tote £100K Guaranteed Placepot Every Day Handicap
quotes.quote: Danger Alert has been an enigma. William knows him very well now and he needs to get there late - the interference probably helped him because otherwise he might have got there too soon - and he puts his head up a little - George Boughey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: You cannot fault him this year as he has already won three times, including last time at Chester over 5f. He never does a huge amount at home so is difficult to gauge, but the important thing is he saves his best for the track. He has shown a liking for slower ground, but I don't think quicker going will be a problem as he's a very versatile. I never saw him getting as high as 94, but he is in good shape and his target next week is the Palace of Holyroodhouse Handicap. 14-06-23

runners.owner: P R Hedger & P C F Racing Ltd
runners.comment: On a good mark but absent since May and this ground might not be optimal
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner; two runs in May were both respectable but he's been absent since; has gone well fresh in the past but whether he wants the ground this testing is a moot point.

quotes.race: BetUK Over 40,000 Live Streamed Races Handicap
quotes.quote: Twilight Madness gave me a really good feel up to halfway but the horse who was making it couldn't really take me far enough and he was dossing a bit once in front - Jason Hart, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Several good runs, including a Windsor win, this year; latest 4th needs bettering though
runners.spotlight: Has won on soft ground but this year's best efforts have come on good to firm; ran okay at Goodwood last month but more will be needed this time.

runners.comment: On dangerous mark & comes here in form; slow ground a query but sire's record offers hope
runners.spotlight: On a dangerous mark now and she has run with credit on her last three starts; cheekpieces return this afternoon (well beaten in them when tried previously); something to prove on slow ground but sire's record on soft/heavy offers hope.

runners.comment: Well treated again now and should be at concert pitch after two runs back from a break
runners.spotlight: Won three sprint handicaps last summer, including over 5f on heavy ground; down the field in two runs back from a break this summer but latest effort was better and he's a leading contender off a reduced mark.

runners.comment: Handicap mark tumbling but it's been hard work in 2025; others are more appealing
runners.spotlight: Handicap mark tumbling but 2025 has generally been a struggle; beaten 4l into fourth at Salisbury on Friday (6f, heavy); unconvincing.

runners.comment: Placed on her last two starts but ground slower than good remains a concern
runners.spotlight: Solid placed efforts in her two starts last month (6f, good to firm and AW); modest form on good to soft and unraced on slower; stable may have stronger claims with Roman Spring.

runners.comment: Won a 6f classified event in July; more recent efforts leave him looking vulnerable
runners.spotlight: Gained an overdue first success in a 6f classified event at Brighton in July (good to firm); went close at Lingfield two weeks later (6f, good to soft) but he's found it tougher on his last three runs; this is easier than last time but others are still more persuasive.

runners.comment: 6f classified win last month; unplaced in two handicaps since and up in class today
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a 6f classified event at Lingfield (good) last month; unplaced in two handicaps since; the ground shouldn't be an issue but he needs more now upped in class.

racecards.verdict: Fiorella Princess is respected but SUB THIRTEEN has slipped in the weights and has conditions to suit.
racecards.betting_forecast: 100/30 Danger Alert, 9/2 Fiorella Princess, 11/2 Twilight Madness, 6/1 Merrimack, 7/1 Sub Thirteen,  South Shore Island,  Mammy, 14/1 Roman Spring, 20/1 Colorada Dancer

racecards.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Led early over this far at Ascot (soft) but trailed home a remote last
runners.spotlight: Drifted out to 28-1 at Ascot (7f, soft) and, having led early, she trailed home a remote last; had to show more.

runners.comment: 55,000euros breeze-up buy out of a 10.6f AW winner (RPR 85); respected newcomer
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros breeze-up buy out of a 10.6f AW winner (RPR 85), closely related to 1m Group 3/smart 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Sea Lord; has to be checked in the betting.

runners.comment: Brother to winners Nakheel (1m2f/14.5f; Group 2; RPR 110) and Elraaed (1m/1m2f; 91)
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to winners Nakheel (1m2f/14.5f; Group 2; RPR 110) and Elraaed (1m/1m2f; 91); dam French/US/Canadian 7f-9.5f winner (95), half-sister to 1m1f/1m2f Group 3 winner Chachnak; in a top yard and the betting should be revealing.

runners.comment: 100-1 for his recent debut over C&D (soft) and pulled too hard to give himself a chance
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling; 100-1 for his recent debut over C&D (soft) and pulled too hard to give himself a chance; best watched for now.

runners.comment: Runner-up to a good filly on her debut here and fully entitled to improve on that form
runners.spotlight: It was a valuable C&D maiden (soft) for fillies in which she was beaten 2l into second behind a favourite who had shown plenty on her Newmarket debut; stable's juveniles usually improve lots for their first run so this Starman half-sister to five winners should be hard to beat.

quotes.race: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Esna -

racecards.verdict: Brian Meehan's ESNA ran to a fair level on her debut here and she'll encounter similar conditions this afternoon.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/6 Esna, 9/4 Morshdi, 11/2 Montague Menace, 16/1 Midnight Bandit, 33/1 Shadowmere

racecards.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Beaten favourite on debut here but finished third after turning for home in last
runners.spotlight: Top-class pedigree as a son of Dubawi and of a winning half-sister to Enable; sent off the 100-30 favourite for last month's C&D debut (soft) and ran on well for a close third after turning for home in last place; the first and fourth horses haven't done wonders for the form since but that was a highly promising start.

runners.comment: 120,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unplaced 1m2f-1m4f (RPR 76)
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unplaced 1m2f-1m4f (RPR 76), half-sister to winners Expand The Map (US 1m) and Sacred Oath (10.6f); stable won this with a newcomer two years ago but this colt will need to be pretty useful to floor Accredit who looks above average.

runners.comment: Improved third at Kempton where he ran on encouragingly from off the pace
runners.spotlight: Both runs over 7f; seventh behind the subsequent Acomb (Group 3) winner at Newbury (good to firm) but finished a lot closer to the runner-up when third at Kempton (AW) where he ran on encouragingly from off the pace; shouldn't remain a maiden for long.

racecards.verdict: It's difficult to get away from the regally bred ACCREDIT who made such a positive start to his career here last month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 1/4 Accredit, 9/2 Plan C, 8/1 Hatteen

racecards.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Fortune Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.comment: Dual novice winner with notable form in handicaps; no easy task on these terms
runners.spotlight: Both wins came early last year in soft-ground novices at Doncaster (7f); has produced some notable efforts in handicaps and raced alone for much of the race when well beaten in the latest at Ascot (hood back on but now discarded); faces no easy task on these terms.

quotes.race: Town Moor Business Club Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We came here today to find out whether we've got a handicapper or a Group horse. That's for the trainer to decide now. But I imagine she's going to look for something flashier - Balmacara could do a bit of damage on soft ground in a Group race.  He's done extremely well from his first run to this, he's come in his coat and he's strengthened up. He did it well last time but he was keen and got tired, today he had a stronger bit in his mouth and raced properly. He loves the ground, I don't think he's dependent on it but when he does get it it will be more of a disadvantage to the others than it will be to him - Charles Bishop, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He won twice and was second twice for us last season. He was third behind Urban Lion at Sandown on his first turf run of the season but he's desperate for it to rain. He's a 10lb better horse when there's juice in the ground - both his wins last year were on soft. So with the weather as it is you probably won't be seeing him for a while, unless I find a race for him in France or Ireland. 02-07-25

runners.comment: Ran right up to his best on favoured soft ground when close up in a French Group 3
runners.spotlight: In and out since showing smart form as a 2yo (beat the top-class Docklands) but loves soft ground and ran right up to his best under those conditions when close up in a Group 3 at Deauville 18 days ago; having to concede weight to 3yos won't be easy but unlikely to be far away.

quotes.race: Coral Challenge (Handicap)
quotes.quote: To go and win today is testament to the horse as Cicero's Gift wasn't fully wound up. The soft ground is important to him as he had bone bruising on all four fetlocks last year. We have a bit of money in the pot now so we may have to supplement him for something - Charles Hills, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been off since the St James's Palace Stakes with a joint problem, but he's done well and is sound now - he's been trotting for the last few weeks and looks great. He's got a lovely mind and this time last year we were really excited about him - he's very talented. He probably won't run until June or July and we'll start in a conditions race over a mile, if one exists. 17-04-24

He was one of the talking horses going into Royal Ascot after winning his first three starts. He isn't very exuberant at home and when you watch him he does nothing flashy. He's so laid back and just does what you ask. We had him out nice and early this year and he won a Wolverhampton novice on his return. That was pretty much what we expected and he then went to Goodwood for a strong conditions race over a mile, which he won very nicely again. On the back of those wins we went for the St James's Palace on Tuesday, although he could only manage seventh on the day behind Paddington. He's a strong, athletic type like his dad, but a little bigger than him. It's an unusual route to this race but on what he's done it was the next logical step. He's clearly effective over a mile but I think he should get 1m2f later on. 21-06-23

runners.comment: Three wins and a Listed defeat; progressive son of Frankel who holds a Group 1 entry
runners.spotlight: Sole defeat came in a Listed race and has otherwise kept a lowish profile, winning novices at Yarmouth and Hamilton before comfortably justifying favouritism on handicap debut back at the latter venue two months ago (extended 1m, good; has won on soft); this Frankel colt holds a Group 1 entry at this trip and retains significant potential.

quotes.race: Darley Stallions EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Sallaal ticks all the boxes. He's very well related to a lot of nice horses, he's a Frankel and that's in him as he's a little bit quirky sometimes, but he's still a big baby and we brought him here hoping he'd race properly and relax, and he did. He realised what his job was in the last furlong. I felt Arctic Grey come to me and we both got racing together down the hill. When I gave him a reminder he really put his head down and galloped strongly through the line, which is what we were all expecting and hoping he'd do - Ray Dawson, jockey.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Sallaal has been a true professional. The only time he wasn't was when they quickened and he couldn't find his legs, but he dealt with it quite well. He showed a bit of class - Jack Mitchell, rider.

stable_tour.quote: There's a lot to like about him. He took all last year to strengthen and wasn't the finished article by a long way when he went to Yarmouth, so I was delighted that he won. He carries all the big entries and is a half-brother to Elmalka and Benbatl, so he's got a bit to live up to. Time will tell if he can match their ability but I think he's quite smart. He's almost ready to run and his first run will tell us a whole lot more. 11-04-25

runners.comment: Listed winner and Group 3-placed; can excuse last two defeats but soft ground a first
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Listed race here last season which remains her career-best form on Topspeed/RPR figures; kicked off this campaign with a second in the Group 3 Nell Gwyn (good to soft) and after flying too high in the French 1,000 Guineas she met with some serious interference in an Irish Group 3 17 days ago; ground this slow will be a first and one or two others appeal more.

quotes.race: Lanwades Stud Nell Gwyn Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: We're delighted with that as Celestial Orbit has been off a while. She should take another step forward from this and she's in the French and English Guineas so we'll make a plan - Ollie Sangster, trainer.

quotes.race: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: We've always liked Celestial Orbit and were just hoping for a piece of the prize-money against some well-fancied horses so for her to win is great. She was an expensive yearling so we're pleased she's managed to pick up some black type. She's laid-back and only does enough in her work but the racing has switched her on and she's done well physically throughout the season. She saw the stiff seven furlongs out well and I think she'll continue to improve. One of the first things Jamie said when he came in is that she should improve over a mile but obviously there aren't many of those races for a bit. She's in the Group 2 Prix du Calvados in August, while the Prestige Stakes could be an option but we'll wait to see how she comes out of this - Ollie Sangster, trainer.

runners.comment: Has run well in Group 1s so major player back at Listed level; should enjoy the ground
runners.spotlight: Debut win was on heavy; managed to hold her own in the 1,000 Guineas and the Coronation Stakes (both on good to firm) before a front-running third in a Group 3 at Ascot in July (1m, good; beaten half a length); major player dropped into a Listed race and could well approve of getting back on slower ground.

stable_tour.quote: I left her in the 1,000 Guineas because her work has been very good. We have a high opinion of and she's trained well. She's enormous - really tall - and we've always been holding on to her. Yet she's very light on her feet and looked very well balanced at Salisbury on her debut. 28-04-25

runners.comment: Dual novice winner who couldn't cope with a French Group 1; can no doubt do better
runners.spotlight: Won novices at about 1m at Wolverhampton and Leicester (good to soft) before finishing well beaten when pitched into Group 1 level for the Prix Rothschild at Deauville (1m, good); it's unlike her trainer to go tilting at windmills so probably has a better chance than her inferior figures may imply.

quotes.race: Nobilo Sauvignon Blanc EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Pina Sonata has grown up an awful lot from her first race to her second and it's good she's been able to follow up Wolverhampton with another nice win. She's a big, strong filly and she's very classy. We're going to have to aim for black type now, but which race to go for I'm not sure. I wanted to get this out the way before we made any real plans. The way she's been going at home has given me the impression she might stay further. She's out of a Galileo mare [Moonlight Sonata] and I'd say she could get a mile and a quarter, but she'll stay a mile for the moment - James Fanshawe, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

racecards.verdict: Chantilly Lace has held her own in Group 1s so has to be feared but the Frankel colt SALLAAL (nap) retains significant potential.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/8 Sallaal, 5/2 Chantilly Lace, 4/1 Cicero's Gift, 6/1 Pina Sonata, 16/1 Celestial Orbit, 20/1 Balmacara

racecards.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap

runners.owner: Mrs Simon Marsh & Hon Mrs Peter Stanley
runners.comment: Slow ground is an unknown but unlucky not to defy 1lb lower on the AW last time
runners.spotlight: Won her first two races on the AW before finishing only seventh when made favourite for handicap debut at Ascot (1m; sole turf start; good to firm); did well to go so close at Kempton last time after fluffing the start and only 1lb higher here; soft ground is a complete unknown, however.

runners.comment: Hosed up on handicap debut; raised 13lb but could well come up with more
runners.spotlight: Shaped okay in maiden and novice races (one on soft) before laughing at her opening handicap mark at Chelmsford (1m; hood removed); the handicapper hit back with a 13lb hike but she could well come up with more.

runners.comment: Non-runner
runners.spotlight: Favourite when winning her second handicap start at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) and 1m2f was perhaps a stretch next time there (again the market leader); not exposed just yet and, although unraced on slow ground, her smart dam handled these conditions; non-runner.

runners.comment: More exposed than some but a dual winner and third here last week on soft ground
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for the Crisfords; kicked off for current yard with 7f wins on good to firm and good; might be a shade high in the weights now but still ran well to be third here last week (again at 7f; soft) and returning to 1m won't faze her.

runners.comment: Non-runner
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Kempton maiden in May but has finished right out the back in her two handicaps, the latest here only last week when well behind Lady Mariko; non-runner.

runners.comment: Potentially on a good mark if tapping back into the promise of last year's sole run
runners.spotlight: Dubawi filly; chased home this season's Nell Gwyn winner on sole run at two (1m AW); well beaten in small fields this year but could yet prove a different proposition in handicaps on this return from a break; market informative.

runners.comment: New cheekpieces; well handicapped on best form and some of that came on slow ground
runners.spotlight: More new headgear tried as she was in a first-time visor when last at Ascot (1m4f) two runs back and again brought up the rear when blinkered at Hamilton (1m1f); well handicapped on best form and some of that came on slow ground.

quotes.race: Enterprising Women Eileen & Jean Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Hackney Diamonds broke smartly and the tailwind behind us took me through the last furlong. I felt like I was on fumes but she was tough and managed to fend them off - Billy Loughnane, jockey, talking to Racing TV.

racecards.verdict: There's a chance that connections have been waiting for soft ground with NAGA and the potential is there to be well handicapped.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Niminy Piminy, 5/2 Maeva, 7/2 Naga, 9/2 Lady Mariko, 10/1 Hackney Diamonds

racecards.race_name: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap

runners.comment: 1-9; only midfield in a Racing League handicap after leaving Andrew Balding
runners.spotlight: Sole win in nine starts came in a 1m AW maiden in March; left Andrew Balding for 28,000gns and was only midfield in a 1m3f Racing League handicap on the AW 20 days ago; with soft ground another cause for concern, he's taken on.

runners.comment: Looks handicapped about right but slower ground might trigger something
runners.spotlight: Debut winner; gelded prior to finishing third in handicaps over C&D (good) and at Kempton (1m3f; both runs in cheekpieces); looks handicapped about right but slower ground might trigger something (withdrawn the once on account of good to firm).

runners.comment: Consistent; only narrowly failed to follow up his Windsor win at Newbury 20 days ago
runners.spotlight: Has been running well in 1m2f/1m4f handicaps and only narrowly failed to follow up his Windsor win at Newbury 20 days ago, again over 1m2f on good ground; 3lb higher here but handles soft ground and looks sure to be involved.

runners.breeder: Mr & Mrs Cotton / Mrs Suzanne Conway
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway
runners.comment: Beaten between about 5l and 13l in his three qualifying runs; likely best watched for now
runners.spotlight: Beaten between about 5l and 13l in his three qualifying runs (1m2f/1m3f; varying ground) and hard to get a handle on what he's achieved; likely best watched.

runners.comment: Only beaten 3.5l at Epsom last time and the winner followed up at Doncaster last weekend
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f on heavy at two when in a different yard; didn't look overly in love with Epsom last time (1m2f, good) and yet was only beaten 3.5l and the winner followed up at Doncaster last weekend.

quotes.race: Best Prices Guaranteed Daily At Vickers.Bet Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Atherstone Warrior jumped and was professional. Kieren [O'Neill, jockey] had a look at the track earlier and we thought it was a little bit better two off the [stands'] rail. Both ours and the Karl Burke horse battled a long way and I think it was just our fitness and bit of experience that caught the other horse out in the end. He's grown a bit since Doncaster and I thought he'd come on for the run  - Alice Haynes, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Not obviously progressive but a handicap debut and the sire loved soft ground
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on from a respectable fifth here on debut and has worn cheekpieces the last twice; however, he's a handicap debutant and his sire excelled on soft ground.

runners.owner: Mrs M V Magnier & S E Sangster
runners.comment: Still unexposed; best RPR came on good to soft and softer here could benefit
runners.spotlight: Plenty of promise in the spring and was returning from a lengthy break when sixth of 11 on handicap debut at Southwell three weeks ago (1m; 11-2); that was a tad underwhelming but it's still early days; best RPR came on good to soft and softer here could benefit.

runners.breeder: Culworth Grounds Farm & Make A Stand Ltd
runners.comment: Unexposed and makes handicap debut in a race his yard has won twice in three years
runners.spotlight: Backward step at Windsor last time (on soft but bred to handle these conditions) but his two previous runs weren't devoid of promise and makes handicap debut in a race his yard has won twice in three years.

runners.comment: First run on slow ground when winning well at Goodwood; 4lb rise could have been worse
runners.spotlight: Closely matched with Sir William on their Windsor clash (good) and this 3yo has since come out and won nicely at Goodwood (1m2f), looking very much at home on the heavy ground; a 4lb rise could have been a lot worse.

quotes.race: Livescorebet Handicap
quotes.quote: The plan was always to go forward but there wasn't much pace and Dancing Tiger found himself in front really easily. He was in his comfort zone throughout, he loves the ground and he galloped out all the way to the line - Marco Ghiani, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

racecards.verdict: A few with chances but SIR WILLIAM has compiled a solid profile in handicaps and can confirm Windsor placings with Dancing Tiger.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Sir William, 7/2 Dancing Tiger, 4/1 Atherstone Warrior, 7/1 Advancing, 10/1 Big Sip,  Perfect Scoundrel, 14/1 The Bellhop, 16/1 Mahogany Bay, 20/1 Jonnie

racecards.race_name: Visit Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap

runners.comment: Progressing but 3lb higher than when beaten last time; unraced on slow ground
runners.spotlight: Stormed clear for a 6l success under Morgan Cole at Epsom (1m2f, good) and kept trying when runner-up under a penalty at Newmarket (good to firm); now 3lb higher and this will be her first run on slow ground, but still appeals as one of the more solid options.

runners.comment: Underwhelming start to career (1m-1m2f); hood added for handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Cost 575,000gns as a yearling; she's made a thoroughly underwhelming start (1m-1m2f) and when third at Nottingham only four ran; again beat just the one home at Goodwood 25 days ago; now makes handicap debut; impossible to know what she'll make of this slower ground and the addition of a hood.

runners.comment: Fifth on handicap debut on the AW, finishing a length behind the more experienced Veraison
runners.spotlight: Only fifth on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f), finishing a length behind the more experienced Veraison; weakened that day after taking a keen hold and this is a stiffer stamina test.

runners.comment: Running okay of late; should be competitive off an easing mark if handling the ground
runners.spotlight: Wolverhampton maiden winner last year over 7f; has finished third in four of her six handicaps (7f-1m2f) but on good ground or the AW and wasn't overly threatening in any of those; should still be competitive off an easing mark if handling the ground.

runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood
runners.comment: 0-5 and has failed to raise her modest profile in handicaps (1m5f/1m2f); more headgear
runners.spotlight: In good hands and has only raced five times, but she's failed to raise her modest profile in handicaps (1m5f/1m2f) and a visor replaces the blinkers tried last time.

runners.comment: Much depends on whether she'll appreciate today's slow ground; pedigree bodes well
runners.spotlight: Has shown modest form and no real progress in her first three races (7f-1m2f); makes handicap debut and much depends on whether she'll appreciate today's first run on slow ground; pedigree bodes well.

runners.comment: One of the better form options after finishing second off this mark at Ffos Las recently
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but with less to prove than some of these after finishing well for second over this far in a Ffos Las handicap three weeks ago (good to soft; unraced on slower); should be in the thick of it off the same mark.

racecards.verdict: This race looks tricky. A chance is taken that HOT TOPIC can improve under these conditions when her pedigree offers hope.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Itraaq, 4/1 Huggable, 5/1 Oceans Charm, 11/2 Veraison, 13/2 Hot Topic, 10/1 Concert Party, 14/1 Amalfina

racecards.race_name: Sea Deer Handicap

runners.owner: Ged Mason, Sir Alex Ferguson, Jim McGoff
runners.comment: Made all (6f, AW) in March; not so good in handicaps and gelded after big defeat in June
runners.spotlight: Bright start when placed in three qualifying runs, including when making all at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in March; has found life tougher in handicaps and was gelded after finishing 24l adrift at York in June; off since and needs to rediscover that initial form; would be a player if he does; first-time tongue-tie.

runners.breeder: G H S Bloodstock & J C Bloodstock
runners.comment: Won maiden on soft; patchy form since March; sold for 17,000gns in July; stable debut
runners.spotlight: Dual winner who won a maiden on soft ground at Redcar for Karl Burke last May; patchy form since winning a Wolverhampton handicap (also 6f, AW) in March and he left George Boughey for 17,000gns after trailing in last of five at Nottingham (5f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie/blinkers) in July; headgear removed for this stable debut.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well at Wolverhampton and was just unlucky to bump into one. He could be out again this week, and he might also go to Lingfield on April 18 for a £65,000 handicap. He's a fun handicapper for the summer. 07-04-25

runners.comment: All runs on AW/sound turf; won maiden (6f, AW) one month ago; similar h'cap form latest
runners.spotlight: All starts on AW or sound turf; off the mark at the ninth attempt when winning a Southwell maiden (6f, AW) one month ago and similar form, despite not looking to quite get home, when third in a 7f Kempton handicap (AW) a fortnight ago; drop back to 6f should help and acts on turf but soft ground would raise a question.

runners.breeder: Ecurie Haras Du Cadran & A Von Gunten
runners.comment: Fetched 28,000gns after a third in Dieppe maiden (5.5f, heavy) in June; stable/h'cap debut
runners.spotlight: Best form from five starts in France when close third of 13 in a Chantilly AW maiden in March; fetched 28,000gns after finishing third in a Dieppe maiden (5.5f, heavy) in June; subsequently gelded; opening mark doesn't look to have left anything to chance but market may help ahead of this stable/handicap debut.

runners.owner: A Bintouq, C Fahy & R & S Marchant
runners.comment: Won h'cap debut (7f, AW) in May; solid since; drop back to 6f/soft a query but respected
runners.spotlight: Gelded before his handicap debut and he rewarded good market support to beat 12 others at Kempton (7f, AW) in May; run of seconds on good/good to firm followed and he wasn't disgraced when sixth of 13 in a Racing League handicap back on AW last time; drop back to 6f and soft ground are imponderables but still respected.

runners.comment: Progressive on AW this year; solid chance if he can transfer that form to turf this time
runners.spotlight: 0-4 on turf but he's proved progressive on AW this year, winning six times including off 4lb lower at Chelmsford in May; good second off this mark at Southwell (also 6f, AW) a fortnight ago; solid chance if he can transfer that form to turf this time.

runners.comment: Turf no problem when 4th (7f) in July; solid AW latest; soft ground a query but considered
runners.spotlight: Lingfield maiden winner in January who showed turf was no problem when fourth, despite pulling hard, on good to firm at Newbury (7f) in July; posted a solid third in first-time cheekpieces at Kempton (6f, AW) last month; soft ground would be an unknown but still needs considering with Oisin Murphy retaining the ride.

runners.comment: Likes it here and won (C&D, good to soft) in May; good 4th in blinkers last month; solid
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW winner whose seven starts here have yielded six places, including when narrowly making his turf breakthrough over C&D (good to soft; visored; Arnaz third) in May; has remained in good nick since and was a close fourth of 14 at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) in first-time blinkers last month; same mark and he's one of the more solid options.

runners.comment: Largely consistent over 6f this year; bumped into one on AW latest; player again.
runners.spotlight: Bitter disappointment at Ascot in May but otherwise consistent over 6f this year, including when he bumped into one on AW 12 days ago; not proving easy to win with off this mark but has run well on soft before and he's a player again.

runners.breeder: J A Knox And Mrs M A Knox
runners.comment: Good third (6f, good to soft) last completed start; pulled out due to soft ground latest
runners.spotlight: Won off 3lb higher at Newmarket (6f, good) just over a year ago so of interest from a handicapping perspective; good third of ten over that C&D (good to soft) on his last completed start but was pulled out at Sandown last time on account of soft conditions, the third time he's been scratched for that reason.

racecards.verdict: This looks trappy, with rain a possibility. The safe option may be DARK SIDE THUNDER who likes it here and has form on good to soft.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Kosometsuke, 9/2 Arnaz, 5/1 Jesse Luc,  Kiniro, 8/1 Dark Side Thunder,  Spring Bloom, 11/1 Wild Clary, 12/1 Hoodie Hoo, 16/1 Jungle Land, 18/1 Tayib Youmzain

racecards.race_name: British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: By Pinatubo; family of G1 winners Fairyland & Dream Ahead; top yard; heed market on debut
runners.spotlight: By Pinatubo; second foal; half-sister to 6f winner Almeraq (including 2yo; RPR 109); dam unraced half-sister to winners Fairyland (5f/6f Group 1 including 2yo) and Now Or Never (Australian 7f Group 2), out of unraced half-sister to 6f/7f Group 1 winner Dream Ahead; holds no fancy entries but that's an eye-catching pedigree for leading stable; market can help on this debut.

runners.comment: Sixth of 11 in Newbury maiden (6f, good) three weeks ago; should be capable of better
runners.spotlight: 625,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; wore a hood (retained) for her introduction and displayed inexperience at the start when 25-1 and sixth of 11 in a Newbury maiden (6f, good) that was dominated by newcomers three weeks ago; form is yet to be tested but she should be capable of better and all of her best siblings won on an easy surface.

runners.comment: Inauspicious start when last of five, beaten 21l, three weeks ago; best watched
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; 9-1 for her debut at Catterick (6f, good) but it was an inauspicious start when last of five, beaten 21l, three weeks ago; stable has much stronger claims with Zighy and she's best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.

runners.comment: Encouraging start when third on AW (5f) 23 days ago; 6f should be fine; turf debut
runners.spotlight: 26,000gns yearling; 11-1 and it was an encouraging debut when third of eight, despite hitting traffic, at Southwell (5f, AW) 23 days ago; shaped like she'd get further that day and not ruled out upped to 6f for this turf debut.

runners.comment: Looks one for nurseries after two modest efforts
runners.spotlight: Green and outpaced over 5f when well held on debut (5f, good to firm) and she took a step backwards when tenth of 12 at Southwell (6f, AW) a fortnight ago; looks one for nurseries.

runners.comment: Drawn widest but eye-catching headway when 6th (6f, AW) 13 days ago; can do better
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; drawn widest and 33-1 for introduction at Southwell (6f, AW) but she made some eye-catching late headway to finish sixth of 12 a fortnight ago; looks capable of better; turf debut.

runners.comment: Blew the start when second on debut; narrow third over C&D last time; sets the standard
runners.spotlight: 400,000gns yearling by Mehmas; all the rage at 4-7 for a five-runner course novice (5f, good) in July but blew the start only to grab second late on; uneasy 13-8 shot but was beaten narrowly when third over C&D (good to firm) one month later; sets the standard.

racecards.verdict: Zighy will likely be off the mark soon but a chance is taken that BINTOLA can build on her promising introduction.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/8 Zighy, 2/1 Albaydaa, 5/1 Bintola, 7/1 Lelosaja, 10/1 Silvery, 25/1 Mad Unicorn, 33/1 Case Study

racecards.race_name: EBF Future Stayers Maiden Stakes (Sire And Dam Restricted Race) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; first foal of a 1m2f winner (RPR 77); hooded for debut; check betting
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; first foal of a 1m2f winner (RPR 77); hooded for debut; only of interest if backed.

runners.comment: Dam a well-related Listed-placed 1m4f winner in France; powerful stable; check betting
runners.spotlight: First foal of a Listed-placed 1m4f winner in France (RPR 99), herself a sister to 1m6f Listed winner Ghostwatch; represents a powerful stable that took this in 2022 (newcomer) and 2019; betting usually a good guide with newcomers from the yard.

runners.comment: 115,000gns half-brother to a winner in France out of a useful mare; betting to guide
runners.spotlight: 115,000gns foal, 115,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to French 1m/8.5f winner Opiana; dam French Listed-placed 1m/1m2f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 93); a market move would look significant.

runners.comment: 145,000euros half-brother to 2 winners out of German 1,000 Guineas winner; appeal on paper
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros foal, 145,000euros yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Lion's Dream (6f/1m2f AW; RPR 85) and Sanafi Zabeel (German 1m1f); dam German 7.5f/1m winner (including Group 2 German 1,000 Guineas; 99); appealing pedigree and he's one to consider.

runners.comment: 750,000gns half-brother to a Grade 3 winner in US; top yard won this in 2017 and 2023
runners.spotlight: 750,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Maltese Falcon (US 8.5f/1m2f including turf Grade 3; RPR 106) and Grey Charger (1m/1m2f including 2yo/AW; 97); dam 1m winner (Listed placed 1m5f AW; 96); stable have taken this race with a newcomer twice since 2017 and he's of obvious interest on paper.

runners.comment: 375,000gns yearling; dam well-related 1m4f winner; in-form yard's 2yos going well in 2025
runners.spotlight: 375,000gns yearling; first foal of a French 1m4f winner, herself a half-sister to French winners Queen's Jewel (1m2f Group 1) and Royalmania (1m1f Listed); in-form stable's 2yos are enjoying a good year; newcomer of note.

runners.comment: Eight winning siblings, four of whom have hit an RPR of 104 or more; lots to like on paper
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; closely related to Group-placed 7.5f AW 2yo winner Golden Crown (RPR 104), half-brother to seven winners including Forest Of Dean (1m2f, Group 3; 116), English Oak (7f/1m; 114) and Crown Of Oaks (1m2f; 105); dam 6f/1m winner (98); lots to like on paper.

runners.owner: Mrs Denis Haynes & Mrs Rebecca Smith
runners.comment: Modest form in two 7f runs this summer; minor nurseries beckon
runners.spotlight: Modest form in her two runs this summer (7f, good to soft and good to firm); minor nurseries beckon.

runners.comment: Dropped away to finish last of ten on last month's Newmarket debut; eyeshield on today
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree but she dropped away to finish last of the ten runners on her Newmarket debut last month (7f, good to firm); likely a longer-term prospect; eyeshield added today.

racecards.verdict: Some interesting newcomers on show and the market can provide clues. PANDEMONIUM is preferred to King Of Berkshire.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 King Of Berkshire, 2/1 Pandemonium, 8/1 Arouse,  Home Secretary, 19/2 Tashi Delek, 20/1 Give Me Sun

racecards.race_name: EBF Stallions John Musker Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
runners.comment: Mostly progressive filly; won 1m4f Listed latest; should be fine back at this trip; player
runners.spotlight: Mostly progressive filly who followed a close third in a York Listed event (1m2.5f, good) in July by making her breakthrough at that level with a narrow but fairly convincing success over 1m4f at that track (good to firm) last month; should be fine back at this trip and she's a threat if soft ground proves no barrier.

quotes.race: BetMGM Winter Oaks Fillies' Handicap (GBBPLUS Race)
quotes.quote: Jack [Mitchell gave Charlotte's Web a great ride from where she was drawn and she was relaxed in front. It's great that we've now won it three times and the plan now is for her to go to All Weather finals day at Newcastle - Simon Crisford, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: BetMGM Winter Oaks Trial Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: It was another step forward for Charlotte's Web and we're very much looking forward to seeing her back for the Winter Oaks. It's too early to compare her to our previous winners because she's still improving. I hope there's more to come from her, and there will need to be to win next time, but I couldn't be more pleased with her attitude and how she finished her race. Her jockey gave her a very nice ride - Simon Crisford, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Won Grade 3 in US a year ago but beaten 12l last two starts; respected on her peak efforts
runners.spotlight: Won a fast-ground Grade 3 at Aqueduct one year ago; fairly encouraging comeback when third of five in the Dahlia at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) in May but beaten 12l despite finishing second of eight over 1m2.5f at York (also Group 2) ten days later; same margin when fourth at Haydock (1m4f) a fortnight later and rested since; respected on her peak efforts but soft ground is an unknown.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: William said they got racing far enough out and the rest of us sort of paid for it. Beautiful Love wants stepping up to a mile and a half but it's just finding the right race because she carries that penalty still from last year - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

quotes.race: Defender 130 (Conditions Race) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Beautiful Love is a very nice filly and when she ran over seven last time it was always going to be too sharp. She'll appreciate stepping up even more to a mile and a quarter and hopefully she's a filly who can keep some nice company back at home - William Buick, jockey.

runners.comment: Made all to win h'cap (1m2f, good to firm) in May; soft ground no bother but plenty to find
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a Nottingham handicap (1m2f, good to firm) in May; came up short in two previous attempts at Listed level, though; inconsistent in cheekpieces on last three starts; soft ground no bother but plenty to find.

quotes.race: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Fillies' Handicap (Jockey Club Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus)
quotes.quote: Ciara Pearl ran in a Listed race last week and probably only had to run to that level. She settled well, caught a breather round the bend and we're rolling on three furlongs out. She's very adaptable, fast ground or slow. They've [joint-trainers Daniel and Claire Kubler] done a remarkable job with her - Jim Crowley, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Renegade Brewery Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: You never know with the ground until they've run. Ciara Pearl has won on soft, but not that sort of ground. She had a chance and I didn't think the first two would pull that far clear of the rest of the field - Daniel Kubler, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Ciara Pearl was very green first time out at Bath and speaking to Cieren (Fallon) this morning, who rode her, he said the mile would probably be her minimum trip at the minute. Around that top bend we were getting slower and slower but she's just got one big stride and that's why I wheeled out and let her use her stride down the hill and she stayed on strongly - Jack Mitchell, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: She's done nothing but progress and ran really well the other day at Chelmsford. She's won seven of her 20 starts. A mile is probably a bit sharp for her, but she does stay and if they got a lot of rain then it could give her an outside squeak. She's been far from disgraced twice in Listed races and handles any going. 10-06-25

She's done nothing but progress and she ran really well the other day at Chelmsford. She's won seven of her 20 starts and she'll go to Royal Ascot for the Kensington Palace. A mile is probably a bit sharp for her, but she does stay and if they got a lot of rain it could give her an outside squeak. If she can run her race then we'll mix and match between some of the valuable fillies' handicaps this season. We hope she can get some black type, which would make her a good prospect as a broodmare. She's been far from disgraced twice in Listed races and she handles any going. 11-06-25

runners.comment: Tailed off at Sandown 18 days ago when soft ground should have suited; risky at present
runners.spotlight: Fourth in this last year; hasn't got going in 2025 and tailed off at Sandown 18 days ago when soft ground should have suited; risky at present.

quotes.race: Market Cross Jewellers British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Molten Rock is a very nice filly. She works well at home and loved the ground today. It was a moderate race but she'll come on a lot for it - Clifford Lee, jockey.

runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen
runners.comment: Fine Listed 2nd (1m2.5f) in July; weakened 1m4f last month; could bounce back at this trip
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly who graduated from handicaps with a fine second of 12 in a York Listed race (1m2.5f, good, Charlotte's Web third, Molten Rock and Modern Utopia behind) in July; prominent in the market when weakening at that track (good to firm) over 1m4f last month; untested on a soft surface but she's the sort to bounce back at this trip for a yard with a solid record in this race.

quotes.race: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% British EBF Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: Rainbows Edge has done it well and she's a very game filly. She's got a great attitude and up to a mile and a quarter would be her best trip - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Mercurial mare; won 1m Gr2 last year; inconsistent but big threat if blinkers fire her up
runners.spotlight: Mercurial mare who is smart on her day; won 1m Group 2 at Royal Ascot and finished a close second in the Group 1 Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft) last year; the effect of the cheekpieces that prompted an improved fourth when bidding to retain her Ascot crown has worn off the last twice, though, including when a surprise market mover but beaten 24l in the Group 1 Nassau (1m2f, heavy) last time; wins nothing on that form, and the widest stall is not helpful, but she'd be a big threat if first-time blinkers fire her up.

quotes.race: Duke Of Cambridge Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: Running Lion has been unlucky. Moves a bit soon in the Dahlia Stakes at Newmarket, gets boxed in at Epsom. We did decide, third time, Oisin, do what you want. He's drawn 11, go to the front, control it. It was perfect, because we got in a muddle in the last two races - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: British EBF 40th Anniversary Upavon Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Running Lion had a bad experience in both the Oaks and French Oaks, when she got sunstroke and the wobbles. I haven't trained her hard for this and she's run a lovely race. I think we've got something to build on now - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: Howden Pretty Polly Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: It will be very interesting with Running Lion if she's more Prix de Diane (French Oaks) or if she's an Oaks mile-and-a-half filly. We'll have to give that a bit of thought. It's lovely to have a filly like this. It's testing ground out there and they've got to be able to handle it. Full marks for her doing it so well. The jockey's first reaction was to possibly stay at a mile and a quarter - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: Racing TV Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was a nice performance. I thought Running Lion showed a great attitude and we'd be happy at a mile for the moment Oisin said, but a mile and a quarter would be no problem in the future. We're delighted with her and she's tough and genuine. We don't have to go anywhere in a hurry; we'll see how she is in the next couple of weeks and take it from there - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Has been struggling, latterly in Salisbury Listed event five weeks ago; work to do.
runners.spotlight: Beat one home in this race last year for different connections and has struggled for her new yard this term, latterly when sixth of eight in the Listed Upavon at Salisbury (1m2f, good to firm) five weeks ago; first-time hood worn there is retained; work to do.

quotes.race: Darley Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was nice. I thought Sea Just In Time did that well. We always thought she was nice and is well-rated, being a sister to Truthful and we had the older sister as well (Sea On Time) who sadly injured a tendon after winning a maiden when she looked nice. There is no rush to decide where she goes next, I'll leave that to William. I think she probably needs three weeks before she runs again and we could look for a Listed contest for her. There are lots of things later in the year like the Irish Oaks. Sea Of Class was second here before going on to win a Listed race and then on to win the Irish Oaks - Maureen Haggas, assistant trainer.

stable_tour.quote: This time last year William Haggas was talking about her as his Oaks filly and when she's good, she's very good. She has two blemishes on her record as well, but on her novice wins she looks a stakes performer. The Nottinghamshire Oaks will be her first target. The Turnbull family bought her and will breed from her eventually and getting black type will be key. 14-04-25

runners.comment: Listed 2nd to subsequent G2 winner (soft, 1m2f) in June; AW latest forgiven; interesting
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a Newbury maiden (7f) for Sir Michael Stoute last year and she was back in form for this yard when second to a subsequent Group 2 winner in a Goodwood Listed race (also soft, 1m2f) in June; weakened when last of six in an AW Group 3 19 days later; plenty to find on the book but far from discounted for her in-form yard.

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Full Of Surprises British EBF Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Anna Swan has the Fillies' Mile entry and would definitely stay. There's plenty of stamina in her family, but there are other options for her, including the Oh So Sharp Stakes on the same card. William was very complimentary about her and she showed she's versatile with the ground - James Savage, assistant to trainer Sir Michael Stoute.

quotes.race: Ruby Sheales Cup Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: It was smooth. We landed into cover, which was very helpful as there's a strong headwind out there. It was a nice opportunity for Anna Swan getting a chunk of weight off a couple of them and she did everything nicely. I've not sat on her before now, I just spoke to James [Savage], our assistant this morning. I wouldn't be pigeonholing her yet on trip, it's always difficult to know how much they'll come on for their first run, but she was pretty professional today - Richard Kingscote, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Progressive filly; went close in a Deauville Group 3 (1m2f, good) last time; big player
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly who finished second in the Listed Pretty Polly at Newmarket (good to firm) in May; went close in a Deauville Group 3 (also 1m2f, good) last time; untried on a softer surface but she's a big player; one of three for a yard that has won three of the last nine renewals.

runners.comment: Good 4th in G2 last year but beaten a total of two horses in three starts this year; risky
runners.spotlight: Really encouraging fourth behind the subsequent 1,000 Guineas winner in last year's May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good) one year ago is starting to fade from the memory and she's beaten a total of two horses in three starts this year; needs to turn the ship around.

stable_tour.quote: We're going to have a look at the May Hill with her after she was an impressive winner on her debut at Newmarket from a tough position. She's a very nice filly in the making and will like a bit of cut in the ground. 11-09-24

She's a very exciting filly. She's done well from two to three and her work is good. She's entered at Newcastle on Friday or she could start in the Prix des Lilas over 1m at Chantilly next month because we have one eye on the Prix de Diane for her. The 1m2f trip could be ideal for her. She ran a good race in the May Hill at Doncaster last year then I ran her on ridiculously heavy ground at Deauville - you can forget that run. 16-04-25

runners.breeder: Airlie Stud & Flaxman Stables Ire Ltd
runners.comment: Won h'caps on testing ground last year; has come up short at Listed level this season
runners.spotlight: Dual handicap winner on testing ground last year who has been trying her hand at Listed level this season; far from disgraced on occasions but a minor role may be the best she can hope for.

racecards.verdict: 3yos have a good record in this race, with Life Is Beautiful and ANNA SWAN making most appeal of that generation.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Running Lion, 7/2 Life Is Beautiful, 9/2 Charlotte's Web,  Beautiful Love, 5/1 Rainbows Edge, 12/1 Anna Swan, 20/1 Modern Utopia, 25/1 Molten Rock, 33/1 Miss Tonnerre, 40/1 Sea Just In Time, 50/1 Ciara Pearl

racecards.race_name: Goffs Two Million Series Handicap

runners.comment: Close to a career best when beaten a head at Goodwood latest; warrants firm consideration
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) at Sandown in May (1m; albeit no match for the winner); well beaten in the Royal Hunt Cup next time but it was close to a career best when beaten a head in 16-runner handicap at Glorious Goodwood (1m1f, good) last month; back up 3lb but he acts on a testing surface and warrants firm consideration.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a cracker at Sandown when second last time. He hasn't had a race in which they've gone really fast yet, but if there's a good pace in the Hunt Cup then he should go well. 10-06-25

He ran really well in the spring and went to Royal Ascot, where he ran really well off top weight, although he was jarred up on the quick ground. On nice ground, 1m2f will be right up his street. 18-09-24

runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who goes well in the mud; bombed out last time but could bounce back
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who goes well in the mud; ran to a good level in his first three handicaps but then suffered a heavy 90l defeat when prominent in the market for the Northern Dancer handicap at Epsom (1m4f, good) on Derby day; absent since, while the experiment with cheekpieces is swiftly abandoned; could bounce back.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet November Handicap
quotes.quote: Valvano was a little bit gassy early and didn't get the breaks. Hector [Crouch, jockey] thought he'd go close a furlong down and he's still learning his job. We'll have another go next year with him - Ralph Beckett, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Valvano was very raw but has a lot of natural ability. Once he was straightened up and knuckled down he showed he's very talented. He was keen on the way to the start and has a bit of a fiery streak in him - he's not short of speed and I wouldn't be in a rush to step up in trip with him, although he will stay a mile and a quarter in time. I hadn't ridden him at home but he's really impressed me here - Hector Crouch, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He had a truncated campaign last year as he was sore after running second to Notable Speech at Kempton early on. He's training well and Saturday's Suffolk Stakes might be a good starting point. He gets a mile and a half but he's got plenty of boot as well. 28-04-25

prev_owners.owner: Sh Rashed Bin Dalmook Al Maktoum

runners.comment: Respectable efforts in UAE in the winter; first start for 194 days; market can help
runners.spotlight: Promising start for this yard when beaten 3l at Meydan in January before winning an Abu Dhabi handicap (1m, good) with more in hand than the winning margin; respectable fifth back at Meydan in February but below that form at that track the following month; first start for 194 days and the market may help to assess his prospects.

quotes.race: Keeper's Heart 10 Year Old Single Malt Whiskey Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Tamrat is one we have liked but we thought he might take one run if not two. The way he stuck his head out was great. He would not want the ground any quicker. He's a big horse and he is one for next year - Shane Lyons, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Exposed handicapper whose recent best has come on AW; safer to look elsewhere
runners.spotlight: Exposed handicapper whose recent best has come on AW; down the field in the Lincoln at Doncaster in March (1m, good to soft) and finished last in the Goodwood's Golden Mile (soft) last time; this is easier but safer to look elsewhere.

quotes.race: BetMGM - Stay Golden With Responsible Play Handicap
quotes.quote: It's always a bit worrying when you've let one go. You're hoping that you'll keep tabs with him but I think we went a decent gallop in our pack in trying to not let the leader get too far away. It's played out and worked in our favour. Fantastic Fox won quite cosily in the end - Aidan Keeley, rider.

quotes.race: MansionBet Bet £10 Get £20 Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: I don't think it is Fantastic Fox's favourite ground, but his class got him through. He's still learning, he's a bit of a boy and is very babyish, and the day he grows up he'll be a nice horse - Silvestre de Sousa, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He ran very well to finish ninth in the race last season and the set-up of it suits him. You wouldn't call him progressive but he is capable of running well at a big price.  09-06-25

A most genuine and likeable colt who won for the second time this year when taking a Haydock handicap this month. It was a memorable day for his owners as Leicester City also won the Community Shield at Wembley. It was also a memorable weekend for the yard as we had six winners from as many runners. Although the ground was very testing that day he seems to be effective in any conditions - it was just that he handled the soft conditions better than his rivals. He's a lovely colt going forward and we'll stick to handicaps for the time being, but ultimately I think he'll evolve into a Cup horse. 18-08-21

runners.comment: Fine spell on AW before form took a plunge on turf in June; mid-div on recent AW comeback
runners.spotlight: Had a fine spell on AW in the first half of the year but his form took a big plunge when last of ten at Epsom (8.5f, good to soft) in June; weakened into mid-division at Kempton (1m, AW; 50-1) 11 days ago on his only start since; might build on that but others are more persuasive.

stable_tour.quote: He's been a revelation through the winter and I was really proud of the way he fought back when two horses went by him at Lingfield on Friday. He's won eight races now and he's not finished yet. 22-04-25

runners.comment: Left Joseph O'Brien for 85,000gns after valuable win in June; of interest on stable debut
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied all starts; winning juvenile who was set to beat this year's dual Derby winner Lambourn until he crashed through the rail towards the finish at Killarney last July; gelded ahead of this year's campaign and it was a career best when landing a seriously valuable event at Gowran (9.5f, good; first-time blinkers) in June; left Joseph O'Brien for 85,000gns the following month; no blinkers for this stable debut but he's an interesting contender.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Gowran Classic
quotes.quote: It's a great result. It's great prize-money and we obviously try to target these types of races. I'm particularly happy for Ronan [Whelan] because he got injured off a filly of ours at the Curragh and cut the back of his head. Today is his first day back riding so it's great for him. Green Triangle got a good set up and we were here to try get some prize-money. I said to Ronan to try and get a positive start and to give him a chance as we had been sending him to the front. He probably enjoyed that and the first-time blinkers - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He contested a smart race at Cork last weekend and just got sucked into it a bit early but did well to finish third. He'll probably go up in distance for a winners' race at Roscommon. He won his maiden there last year so it looks a suitable spot for him. 24-04-25

runners.comment: Good runs in 1m h'caps the last three times; will likely give his running again; respected
runners.spotlight: Gelded after his maiden success in June; good efforts in three 1m handicaps since, including when beaten around 1l at Glorious Goodwood (heavy) and at York's Ebor meeting (good to firm); will likely give his running again and commands respect.

runners.comment: Rallying 4th at York (1m, good to firm) last time; best form on a good or quicker surface
runners.spotlight: Hasn't always had the breaks this year and that was the case again when a rallying fourth in the Clipper at York (1m, good to firm) last time; all his best form has come on a good or quicker surface, however, and needs conditions to dry out to be seen to best effect.

runners.breeder: G H S Bloodstock & J C Bloodstock
runners.comment: Winning reappearance (1m) in May; flopped on h'cap debut; by no means one to give up on
runners.spotlight: Firms steps forward in each of his first three runs and made a winning reappearance at Haydock (1m, good) in May; made favourite for his handicap debut at Chester (1m2.5f, good) only to flop in July, when his trainer felt conditions were too quick; gelded and rested since and he's by no means one to give up on.

racecards.verdict: Plenty to consider but the vote goes to LOVE BEACH, who had an excuse on his handicap debut and is the least exposed in the field.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Sea Force, 11/4 Whip Cracker, 11/2 Green Triangle, 6/1 Valvano, 10/1 Mythical Guest,  Love Beach, 12/1 Fantastic Fox, 14/1 Tamrat, 25/1 Mr Baloo

racecards.race_name: British EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Consistent last four starts; step up in trip may suit; acts on an easy surface; solid
runners.spotlight: Doubled her tally when scoring at Haydock in April (1m2f, good) and consistent when placed on last four starts; has sometimes shaped like this step up in trip will suit; acts on an easy surface; respected.

runners.comment: Won 1m4f novice on soft; yet to find stride this year; not ruled out from falling mark
runners.spotlight: Sole win in nine runs came in a 1m4f novice on soft ground; yet to find her stride this year but she's not ruled out from a falling mark.

runners.comment: Made all (1m2f, good) last Tuesday; 6lb penalty; quick turnaround a query but respected
runners.spotlight: Made all to win at Leicester (1m2f, good) last Tuesday for which she incurs a 6lb penalty; unexposed after just six starts but her career has been punctuated by mini-breaks so this quick turnaround does asks a question; untested on soft but this trip could unlock more.

stable_tour.quote: She was a bit disappointing at Southwell under a penalty as I thought she'd take the beating that day, but she came back and she was heavily in season, so she's had a break. I'm glad there was something to excuse her below-par effort because her work was decent and I'm looking forward to seeing her make her turf debut in the next few weeks. 07-04-25

runners.comment: Won h'cap debut at 1m4f (good to firm); step backwards next time; still early days
runners.spotlight: Only had to be pushed out for a successful handicap debut at Doncaster on her first attempt at 1m4f (good to firm) in June but took a step backwards under similar conditions when raised in class at Salisbury last time; still early days.

runners.comment: Soft-ground family; won AW h'cap (1m3f) last time; 4lb higher but could be on the up now
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground loving family and she showed plenty of promise in testing conditions as a juvenile; made the breakthrough when keeping on well in a Kempton AW handicap (1m3f) last time; 4lb higher but could be on the up now.

stable_tour.quote: She's from a family we know well given we trained her dam and she's related to Madame Chiang, who won for us on Champions Day in 2014. She's done everything right. 04-09-24"

prev_owners.owner: C Benham/ L Quinn/ K Quinn

runners.comment: Emphatic (1m2f, soft) last time; up 7lb; return to this sort of trip no issue; respected
runners.spotlight: Fresh contender, having raced only twice this term; registered an emphatic win at Windsor (1m2f, soft) last time, running on well to score by over 4l; up 7lb but return to this sort of trip should be no issue; respected.

runners.comment: Fifth of six on h'cap debut (1m4f, good) left plenty to be desired; question to answer now
runners.spotlight: Readily won Lingfield maiden (good to soft) in June before disappointing favourite backers under a penalty at Nottingham (also 1m2f, good) having raced freely the following month; fifth of six on handicap debut at Goodwood (1m4f, good) left plenty to be desired last time; question to answer now but too soon to write her off.

runners.comment: Opening mark isn't a disaster but bit more needed on h'cap debut; no means unlikely
runners.spotlight: All three starts on AW around this trip; marginally the best of those qualifying runs came when fourth of eight at Kempton (1m4f) last time; opening mark isn't a disaster but bit more needed, which is by no means unlikely.

racecards.verdict: A few to consider but MISS WONG (nap) got off the mark last time and her pedigree suggests that was the start of something better.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 North Star, 7/2 Miss Wong, 4/1 Salamanca City, 9/2 Ghost Story, 9/1 Camtank, 10/1 Lady La Fay, 12/1 Thankyou Baroness, 14/1 Shaffron

racecards.race_name: QuinnBet Fillies' Handicap

runners.breeder: Davis Mitchell & Lope De Vega Syndicate
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough in 14-runner AW handicap latest; 2nd won next time; big player
runners.spotlight: Has been advancing her form in handicaps and she made the breakthrough when scoring narrowly in 14-runner handicap at Southwell (6f, AW) three weeks ago; up 4lb for that short-head win but the second was a comfortable winner next time; big player if potential soft ground proves okay.

runners.comment: Won penultimate start; not so good next time and this mark looks tricky.
runners.spotlight: Scored for the third time when winning at Pontefract (6f, good to firm) on her penultimate start; up to a mark that's proved problematic before and she could beat only one home at Kempton next time (AW) next time.

stable_tour.quote: She won at 50-1 on her first run at Wolverhampton, over 7f, in early April. I paid very little money for her but I just liked her as she was walking around the paddock. She had a little bit of an ugly right-handed ankle but that didn't bother me as I bought her off my own bat and turned her out for two months. She has very good owners in Premier Racing, who own 60 per cent of her with two of my other owners. They've had five horses with me and they've all won- three of them, including her, won first time out. The thing is that by winning first time out the handicapper will take a dim view of that, so my view, like my dad's, is that you're better off going to a decent fillies' race, so we ran her in a 6f Listed race at Chelmsford last week. 08-05-24

runners.comment: Struggles for consistency; lesser effort latest; first run at 6f for over two years
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish soft/heavy-ground winner; 0-10 for James Owen since June; has posted some good efforts in defeat but struggles for consistency and it was one of her lesser efforts when fifth of seven at Chester (7f, good) on Friday; first run at 6f for over two years.

runners.comment: Consistent in handicaps; drop back to 6f when solid 3rd (AW) last time; mark fine; player
runners.spotlight: In largely consistent form since handicapping, form figures of 6243 including a runner-up effort here (7f, good) in July; drop back to 6f didn't stop a solid effort when third at Lingfield (AW) last time; mark fine; player.

runners.comment: Built on handicap debut when second a fortnight ago; drop back to 6f looks interesting
runners.spotlight: Last three starts on AW; built on her handicap debut when second of seven at Lingfield (7f), despite racing freely, a fortnight ago; drop back to 6f could be the way forward and Oisin Murphy takes over; interesting.

runners.comment: Yet to be placed in six starts for this yard; others are more persuasive
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins on AW for Ivan Furtado last spring; yet to be placed in six starts for this yard and was never competitive in two starts in June; handicap mark makes her of interest but others are more persuasive.

racecards.verdict: Vinatera, Ironist and ADDARELLA may all have their best days ahead of them with the vote going to the latter on the drop to 6f.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Vinatera, 3/1 Addarella, 7/2 Little Keilee, 4/1 Ironist, 10/1 Court Drive, 14/1 The Real McKay

racecards.race_name: Chung Hom Kok Handicap (Handicap) (Class 5) (3yo+) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.owner: Janet Wong Hoi Shan, John Yuen Se Kit Et Al
runners.comment: Dropped in class and from 6f with Purton aboard for the first time; interesting.
runners.spotlight: Yet to improve upon 2l fourth of his January 6f debut in six subsequent tries (all at that trip) but combination of drop in grade and distance makes him interesting; now 12lb lower than his opening mark and Zac Purton rides.

runners.comment: Recent trial winner who suffered leg injury in April; down in class with good claimer up.
runners.spotlight: Recent trial race success suggests that he has recovered from injury sustained in April; has made the first five in four of his last six starts; first try down at Class 5 with top apprentice booked.

runners.comment: Lost form badly late last season; would have a squeak if reproducing last autumn's runs.
runners.spotlight: Has posted seven poor efforts since breaking his duck at 16th attempt off 2lb higher mark last December (5f, Sha Tin); back from 101-day break here.

runners.comment: Beaten only 1.75l on recent comeback and this drop to 5f could be in his favour.
runners.spotlight: Helped force the pace and only weakened late when 1.75l fifth on comeback ten days ago; this drop to 5f could suit and he boasts some fair form in higher-class races.

runners.owner: Ng Chong Muk, Wong Man & Ng Siu Luen
runners.comment: Has won on his comeback before but better at 6f and drawn wide.
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f course winner off slightly higher marks who is returning from injury; may lack the speed for 5f but did win first time out last season; poorly drawn.

runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Chan Hou Bo & Mr & Mrs Edwin Chan
runners.comment: Will probably make the running but back from 189 days off so fitness a big question mark.
runners.spotlight: Has twice made all over this C&D but is returning from injury and needs to show much more than he did on his solitary 2025 run, over this C&D in March.

runners.breeder: Newlands House Stud & Mrs A M Burns
runners.owner: Bernard Man, Stephen Man Shiu Fai & Gerard Man
runners.comment: Bled on last start two months ago; his best placed form is all over further than this.
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in 21 tries but was sent off favourite when only seventh (later found to have bled) when last seen in July; has run well fresh; this trip may be too short.

runners.comment: Twice a C&D scorer and lots of placed form late last season; one for the shortlist.
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner off slightly higher marks than this in 2024; placed on three of his last four so is on a reasonable mark and cannot be discounted.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Auction Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Special Power has been the cause of his own downfall a few times but he got it right tonight. If he's alright we might go to Naas on Sunday for the Birdcatcher - Andy Slattery, trainer

runners.comment: First run for new trainer; glimmer of hope in two recent trials; official form is poor.
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish closer than seventh in nine starts to date but has moved trainer since his last appearance in April and twice runner-up in recent trial races.

runners.comment: Yet to make the top four but was only beaten by 1.25l on his solitary run over this C&D.
runners.spotlight: Best effort from 11 career starts came on his only try over this C&D when 1.25l fifth off 8lb higher mark than this in April; has regressed in two runs since.

runners.comment: Old-timer who seems to have lost his speed but does have two C&D wins to his name.
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has managed just a single third in 13 starts since winning off a 16lb higher mark over this C&D 11 months ago; needs to have been rejuvenated by his summer break.

runners.comment: Has looked moderate in 13 starts to date; dropping back to 5f for the first time here.
runners.spotlight: Has yet to make the top four in any of his 13 career outings and needs this first try at 5f to have a big impact if he is to get competitive; first run for 84 days.

runners.comment: Winless for almost a year but likes this C&D; first run for new trainer; reserve.
runners.spotlight: Will be making his debut for new trainer if getting a run today; twice C&D victor off this kind of mark and was a fair second over 6f here on his penultimate start.

runners.comment: Was upped 6lb for his short-head C&D triumph in June; unraced since; reserve.
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of his reduced rating to get off the mark at the tenth attempt over this C&D when last seen in July; raised 6lb for that victory,.
