racecards.verdict: Punters may have their heads turned by the booking of Zac Purton for Golden Friendship but preference is for NOBLE DELUXE.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Golden Friendship, 7/2 Noble Deluxe, 4/1 Modest Gentleman, 15/2 Silver Spurs, 14/1 Triumphant Warrior, 16/1 Spicy So Smart, 20/1 Cosmo Navigator,  Happy Horse,  Camp Fire, 25/1 Wisdom Star,  Happy Tango, 33/1 The Prime

racecards.race_name: Hoi Mei Handicap (Div I) (Class 4) (3yo+) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.comment: Very little to enthuse about in any of his four Hong Kong appearances thus far.
runners.spotlight: Has started at huge odds and finished tenth or worse in all four Hong Kong starts; others preferred.

prev_owners.owner: A Boyd, P M Gangemi & Talinga Girls

runners.comment: Beaten by under 2l in each of his last two runs; on a workable mark but may need further.
runners.spotlight: His only victory in 16 career outings came over 1m here in February; remains 2lb above his winning mark and this trip may be on the sharp side; won a recent trial.

runners.breeder: M W Freeman & D G Price
runners.comment: Winner of two of his last three (both this C&D) and 7lb rise does not seem overly harsh.
runners.spotlight: Following 17 straight defeats, has won two out of three (both this C&D) since joining this stable; won with more in hand than 1.25l margin suggests last time so 7lb rise appears fair.

runners.comment: Best three performances in nine career attempts were all over this C&D; merits respect.
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish in the first two in nine Hong Kong starts but has been beaten by 2.25l or less in all three attempts over this C&D; worth considering off 6lb lower than for any of those races.

runners.comment: Has showed some ability in each of his last two starts; not one to give up on just yet.
runners.spotlight: All three career starts have come over this C&D and he would not need too much improvement from the best of them (3.5l fourth off 4lb higher mark) to get involved in the finish here.

prev_owners.owner: The Exors Of The Estate Of The Late Howard Liang Yum Shing

runners.comment: Ran well enough when second on penultimate run (6f, Sha Tin) to merit consideration here.
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; latest run was a step backwards but previous 1.5l second at Sha Tin showed that he can run well fresh; cannot be discounted in this weak affair.

runners.comment: Hard to fancy on the back of three underwhelming effort (6f & 5f) thus far.
runners.spotlight: Beaten by at least 7l in all three career starts; would need to make a big leap forward to get competitive today.

runners.comment: Close sixth on his debut but has fared less well twice since; off since February.
runners.spotlight: Only beaten by 2.5l on his 5f debut in December but has run less well in just two appearances since; that initial run did at least suggest that he can get fit without racing.

runners.owner: Chan Kam Shing, Wu Kai Char Et Al
runners.comment: Has three C&D victories to his name and won a similar race on his comeback last season.
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D scorer (all off higher marks than this) who has been beaten 12 times (three places) since winning on his seasonal debut a year ago; may been refreshed by his summer break.

runners.owner: Cheung King Shek, Billy Cheung King Shan Et Al

prev_owners.owner: Pulse Dream Genie, P M O'Shea Et Al

runners.comment: Has a C&D success to his name as recently as May but three runs since have been modest.
runners.spotlight: Finally got his head in front at the 19th attempt over this C&D in May; his three subsequent efforts were only fair and he remains 4lb above that successful mark.

runners.comment: In poor form late last season but summer break may have helped; three-time winner.
runners.spotlight: Only victory in 22 starts since landing his first two races was over 5f here off 5lb lower mark ten months ago; stays 6f and possible that a 74-day layoff since his last run will have helped him.

runners.breeder: S F Bloodstock L L C
runners.comment: Two of his three best runs have been over C&D; could go well in first time cheekpieces.
runners.spotlight: Still searching for first success after 11 attempts; twice runner-up (beaten by 0.5l or less) over C&D in May before two lesser runs; dropped 3lb since; cheekpieces applied.

runners.breeder: A D, J D & Mrs L J E Scoular
runners.comment: Twice a 6f winner; dropped out late on recent comeback; should be fitter now; reserve.
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f scorer who ended last season with three straight top-four finishes; should have come on for his recent seasonal debut and merits respect if getting a run.

runners.breeder: Miss J M, K J & Mrs M L Shaw
runners.comment: Last of 14 on comeback ten days ago and best form is all over further than this; reserve.
runners.spotlight: All his best efforts since a 7f win a year ago have come on dirt and he's likely to lack the speed to be effective over 6f; plum last on recent 7f comeback.

racecards.verdict: A third C&D victory in his last four starts is on the cards here for GOLDEN DARCI with Know At Heart likely to give him most to do.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Golden Darci, 6/1 Golden Luck, 15/2 Always My Folks, 8/1 Mighty Commander, 9/1 Know At Heart, 10/1 California Blitz, 12/1 Amazing Kid,  Telecom Power, 16/1 Champion Method, 25/1 Snowthorn, 33/1 Star Glow,  Rewarding Buddy

racecards.race_name: Lido Handicap (Class 4) (3yo+) (Course B) (Turf)

prev_owners.owner: Mrs M E Baker, Mrs R E Nicholson Et Al

runners.comment: Has dropped to a fair mark and may have benefited from recent seasonal debut.
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner including over this trip; has dropped a little in the ratings since a string of fair efforts late last season and may have needed recent comeback run; poorly drawn.

runners.owner: Rotary Club of Sha Tin Syndicate
runners.comment: Classy in Brazil; edging towards overdue first local win; down another 3lb since last run.
runners.spotlight: Group 1 winner in Brazil but 0-20 in Hong Kong; suggested he was about to hit form with a pair of placed efforts in the spring; summer break may have prompted further progress.

runners.breeder: J S Alexander, W Bevins Et Al

prev_owners.owner: Ciaron Maher Racing, M Tsang Et Al

runners.comment: Not one to rely upon but has a chance if reproducing form of two April C&D second places.
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent (strike-rate of 2-40) but has two course wins to his name and was twice second over this C&D in April prior to a pair of lesser efforts.

runners.comment: His two wins have come at 1m and handicapper may just have his measure at present.
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye of the stewards when lacking room in late stages of 4.25l sixth on recent comeback; still 1lb higher than for latest win here in December (1m).

runners.comment: Front-runner who has gone up 13lb for two recent wins; revised mark makes things tough.
runners.spotlight: Three-time 1m1f winner, most recently when a 19-1 chance at Sha Tin on latest run in June; 7lb rise for that means that he needs to improve further; front-runner.

prev_owners.owner: R T D Racing, Flopsyandfriends Et Al

runners.comment: Hard to recommend having finished outside of the top eight in all seven Hong Kong outings.
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish better than ninth in seven tries since imported from Australia; needs to have turned a corner in the 112 days since his last run.

runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Jerry Chow Shiu Ming
runners.comment: Likely to find this trip too short and is still 8lb above the mark of his solitary win.
runners.spotlight: Only success from 18 tries came over 1m3f here in May; remains 8lb above his winning mark and drop in distance here is not in his favour.

runners.comment: Irregular turf winner but only 2lb higher than when touched off (1m1f, Sha Tin) in July.
runners.spotlight: His strike-rate on dirt (2-14) is much better than on turf (1-23) but he was only touched off by a head when last seen in July and has changed trainer since.

runners.comment: Recent return will have blown cobwebs away; has gone close on both previous C&D tries.
runners.spotlight: Placed on both C&D attempts, including off this mark in June, and may come on for his recent comeback outing, when he was only beaten by 3l into seventh; Zac Purton has a 1-3 record on him.

runners.comment: Has slipped down to a good handicap mark but 1m1f may be too short for him.
runners.spotlight: Defeated in all 11 starts since 1m3f win here 19 months ago, hence his rating in 6lb lower now, but probably needs a longer trip than this.

runners.comment: Prolific Macau scorer; has struggled since shipping to Hong Kong; poor recent comeback.
runners.spotlight: Win six times in Macau (three over this trip) before modest start to his Hong Kong career; ran a couple of good races at the end of last season but recent reappearance effort lacked zest.

runners.breeder: Gestut Zur Kuste Ag & Riviera Equine
runners.comment: Came close to ending long win drought late last season; may prefer slightly shorter.
runners.spotlight: Without a win since May 2024 but has slipped a little in the weights as a result and been placed in each of his last three starts; fair claims provided 1m1f does not stretch his stamina.

quotes.race: KPMG Champions Juvenile Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: Atomic Jones is a real horse for next year over a mile plus. He's a big huge baby and I didn't expect to be running him this year, never mind winning two - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Atomic Jones came here a big raw horse but obviously he was good enough to do that. Colin said he loved the ground and he will be a trip horse for next year - Shane Lyons, trainer.

runners.owner: Christina Y C Ting, Chen Wen Bin Et Al
runners.comment: Out of form in recent months but would be well weighted if back to his best; reserve.
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in his last six starts but is well handciapped if judged on 1l second on last year's seasonal return (1m, Sha Tin).

runners.owner: Charley Chan Wing Chin & Li Kit
runners.comment: Only success was at odds of 84-1 over 7f; rest of his form is modest at best; reserve.
runners.spotlight: Only top-three finish from 11 attempts was a shock 84-1 victory over 7f at Sha Tin in February; highly inconsistent; first run at beyond 1m.

racecards.verdict: Having showed a liking for this C&D with two good efforts, FIREFOOT can get the better of Supreme Agility.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Firefoot, 5/1 Atomic Beauty, 6/1 Kingly Demeanor, 7/1 Forever Folks, 15/2 Supreme Agility, 9/1 Samarkand, 12/1 Can't Go Wong,  Romantic Fantasy, 14/1 Prince Alex,  Perfect Pairing, 20/1 Yiu Cheung Victory, 33/1 Dilbagh

racecards.race_name: Community Chest Cup (Handicap) (Class 3) (3yo+) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.comment: C&D specialist who rarely runs a bad race; narrowly beaten off 2lb higher mark a week ago.
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D scorer who made a fine start to the season when losing out in a three-way photo at long odds a week ago; should go well again off 2lb higher mark.

runners.comment: Still 1lb above latest winning mark but capable of a big run if he is on a going day.
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and has not won any of his last ten starts but has four C&D verdicts under his belt and finished 0.5l second on penultimate outing.

runners.comment: Has finished in the top three in over 40 per cent of his 50 starts; often goes well fresh.
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner including off a 1lb higher mark on his seasonal debut 12 months ago; inconsistent since but has a good record when fresh.

runners.comment: C&D winner in March, afterwards twice runner-up of this kind of mark; chance again here.
runners.spotlight: Remains 7lb above the mark of his solitary local success (from ten runs), which came over this C&D in March; needs to find a bit more if he is to return to winning ways.

runners.comment: High class in New Zealand; finally managed first local wins last season; goes well fresh.
runners.spotlight: New Zealand Derby winner so his 2-34 record in Hong Kong is disappointing; won twice at this venue last season, though, and appears best fresh nowadays; first run in 63 days here.

runners.comment: Had two of these narrowly behind in C&D win in June; worse off at the weights here.
runners.spotlight: Came good with two victories (7f/1m, at odds of 16-1 and 10-1) last season but is not weighted to confirm the form of latest success with two of these rivals.

runners.comment: Twice a narrow 1m1f winner here in April; lesser runs since including on recent comeback.
runners.spotlight: Only 0.75l behind Another Zonda over this C&D in June and is now 8lb better off at the weights; only beat two on recent reappearance run but may be fitter now.

runners.comment: Win and two places in five tries since joining current yard; first try at beyond 6f.
runners.spotlight: Course winner but has never been asked to go further than 6f in 12 Hong Kong outings; staying-on sixth on recent comeback; this trip may be beyond him.

runners.breeder: G W & Mrs M W Simon
runners.owner: Star Chan Iu Seng, S Chan Cham Man & Winnie Chan Wing Yan

prev_owners.owner: Star Chan Iu Seng, Simon Chan Cham Man Et Al

runners.comment: Consisent but 4-40 win ratio is unremarkable and he has nothing in hand at these weights.
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D scorer who ran well in all but three of his 11 outings last term; that consistency means he remains 5lb above his highest winning mark, though.

racecards.verdict: Back to winning ways last term after a long drought, ROCKET SPADE may just get the better of last week's close C&D third I Can.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 I Can, 100/30 Star Contact, 11/2 Excellence Value, 6/1 Rocket Spade, 9/1 Another Zonda, 10/1 Romantic Laos, 12/1 Victor The Rapid,  Sure Joyful, 14/1 Harmony Galaxy

racecards.race_name: Hoi Mei Handicap (Div II) (Class 4) (3yo+) (Course B) (Turf)

prev_owners.owner: Newgate Bloodstock, China Horse Club Racing Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.comment: Went close over C&D one week ago and has the plum inside draw again; good chance
runners.spotlight: Returned to form and lost out only by a neck over C&D when dropped to this grade for last week's seasonal debut; still on a good mark after subsequent 2lb nudge and has the plum inside draw again; good chance.

runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Tser Yiu Cho, J Tser Ka Ho & Johnson Tser Ka Yu
runners.comment: Ended last season in good form but faces tough task from stall 11 today
runners.spotlight: Made all over C&D in June and ended last season with two perfectly respectable runs off today's mark but faces a tough task from stall 11 today.

runners.owner: Cheung Tin Chai, Cheung Shu Yan & Cheung Shu Tak
runners.comment: Ended last season in career-best form and is well drawn today; one to consider
runners.spotlight: Led close home to score over C&D in May and bettered that form when going very close on final two outings last season; well drawn for this reappearance outing and has obvious claims if still in same form.

runners.owner: Arthur Antonio Da Silva, Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva
runners.comment: 0-5 but yet to run a bad race; likely to be on the premises again under Zac Purton
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after five runs but has made the frame four times, most recently when short-head second over C&D at end of last season; Zac Purton keeps the ride; likely to be right in the thick of things again.

runners.comment: Well down the field all six Hong Kong starts and difficult to enthuse over
runners.spotlight: Well down the field all six Hong Kong starts (5f-1m) and difficult to enthuse over.

runners.owner: Cheung Ka Shing & Jason Cheung Cho Yee
runners.comment: Did not progress after C&D win last November and ended last season in poor form
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last November, his second start, but is yet to make any significant further progress and his efforts towards the end of last season were particularly disappointing.

runners.comment: Made reasonably encouraging debut in July but others have more pressing claims
runners.spotlight: Made reasonably encouraging debut when eighth over 5f here in July but others in this line-up have more pressing claims.

runners.comment: Unplaced all nine Hong Kong starts and has stall 12 for this seasonal/stable debut
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed after nine Hong Kong starts, and signed off for David Hayes with a poor performance over 1m in May; drops back in trip for seasonal/stable debut; looks up against it from stall 12.

runners.comment: Useful sprinter when based in Ireland but unplaced all ten HK starts; tries blinkers
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinter when based in Ireland but unplaced all ten Hong Kong starts (7f-1m); may benefit from combination of drop back in trip and first-time blinkers today but has a lot to prove.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Buyin Buyin was strong at the finish and looked like he might get another furlong. He was workmanlike rather than spectacular but I'll take it. Hopefully we might run him in the Anglesey Stakes in a couple of weeks. I think he deserves to go and whether he is good enough we're not sure - Willie Browne, trainer.

runners.comment: 0-8 but did well to finish second over C&D from stall 11 in June; each-way claims
runners.spotlight: A maiden after eight runs but did well to finish second over C&D from stall 11 in June and was denied a clear run when only ninth here on final start last season; 2lb lower today and considered each-way.

runners.comment: Dual Class 5 dirt winner last season; vulnerable on turf in this grade from stall 10
runners.spotlight: Dual Class 5 dirt winner last season (6f); not beaten far on either turf start since returned to this grade but has double-digit draw today and will probably remain vulnerable at this level.

runners.comment: 15-race maiden; begins new season on a tempting mark and can't be ruled out

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Mill Reef Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: Mentally, there's a bit more improvement in Wings Of War. He was a little bit on edge when he got here, but as the day went on he got more and more at ease with himself and put in a performance to be proud of - Clive Cox, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's coming to the boil at the right time and I'm thrilled with him. His run at Ascot in the Pavilion was very much a first-time-out effort and he finished with great zest in the Sandy Lane. He's come back from that extremely well and this has been the target all winter. 09-06-22

He enjoyed a fine juvenile season, which culminated with him winning the Group 2 Mill Reef Stakes. First time back in the Pavilion at Ascot he was still a bit rusty and had that Group-race penalty. But he ran a much better race last time when third to El Caballo in the Group 2 Sandy Lane Stakes. I feel he's coming to the boil in time for the Commonwealth Cup. Mentally he has matured, physically he's much stronger and I'm delighted with how he came back from Haydock. 09-06-22

runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo with 3-7 strike-rate (all wins gained over the straight 5f at Sha Tin); safely held on final two starts last season and untested on this track but Zac Purton keeps the ride; a possible.

runners.spotlight: Without a win since 1m1f Group 3 handicap success in November 2023 and was out of form for David Hall in second half of last season; needs to have been revitalised by switch to new stable.

stable_tour.quote: When the yearlings come in you can usually spot the nice ones straightaway and this fellow stood out in the pack. I like his sire and he has left his stamp on this colt who is physically gorgeous, sound, strong, a good mover and has an excellent temperament. This fellow made a very pleasing debut at Haydock in May and it did him the power of good. He came forward for that and I expected a big run last week at Doncaster, when he did not disappoint with a 4L win. Potentially he could be anything as our juveniles usually come on a lot for their initial efforts. The plan immediately after that was the Superlative Stakes this week but I spoke to his owner afterwards and he is a very patient man with his horses and suggested we may wait a little longer, in which case the Listed Pat Eddery Stakes at Ascot on 24 July would be the target. This son of Churchill has always looked a cut above my others and the lads that have ridden him at home have all said he feels like a proper horse. I think he has a very bright future and could be anything. I know it is early days but he could be very smart indeed. 07-07-21

runners.comment: Won twice over C&D in February and was placed another four times before end of last season
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D in February and was placed another four times before end of last season; not ideally drawn today and has little margin for error off current mark but still has a part to play under Zac Purton.

quotes.race: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
quotes.quote: Ideally a really hard mile is what Connemara Coast wants, when he's almost off the bridle behind, and then he'll outstay them, but he'll definitely be a mile and quarter horse next season. He might go to the July meeting but Ryan thought there'd be an Ascot handicap for him at the end of the year. When they go flat out at Ascot it will be perfect for him - Richard Hughes, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He has never run a bad race in his six starts to date, which includes a win at Wolverhampton last autumn and at Sandown on his most recent outing. Ryan [Moore] won on him at the Esher track and it is always great to get him, but he is a man in serious demand and it is not that easy to get him on one of yours. He is now on a 3lb higher mark of 86 and the plan is to head back to either Newmarket for the July meeting next month where there is a three-year-old 0-95 mile handicap, or Ascot a day later for a 0-95 for three-year-olds. We will try to get Ryan again as he remains on a very workable mark. 29-06-23
